articles.headline: RBI to pay record ₹2.11 lakh crore dividend to Government
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India board approved a record surplus transfer of ₹2,10,874 crore for FY24. This is substantially higher than the budgeted ₹1.02 lakh crore, providing a significant fiscal boost to the government for the current financial year.

articles.headline: Sensex and Nifty hit record highs on RBI dividend boost
articles.summary: Indian equity indices Sensex and Nifty touched all-time highs following the RBI's record dividend announcement. The Sensex surged 1,196.98 points to close at 75,418.04, while the Nifty 50 climbed 369.85 points to settle at a record 22,967.65.

articles.headline: IndiGo Q4 profit doubles to ₹1,895 crore on strong demand
articles.summary: InterGlobe Aviation reported a 106% year-on-year increase in its fourth-quarter net profit to ₹1,894.8 crore. The airline's revenue from operations rose 26% to ₹17,825.3 crore, driven by a 14% increase in passenger traffic and improved yields.

articles.headline: Political parties shift focus to Phase 6 of Lok Sabha elections
articles.summary: Campaigning intensified for 58 seats across eight states and UTs voting in the sixth phase on May 25. Key leaders from the NDA and I.N.D.I.A. bloc held rallies in Delhi, Haryana, and West Bengal, focusing on economic policies and constitutional rights.

articles.headline: Adani Group market cap nears pre-Hindenburg levels after surge
articles.summary: Shares of Adani Group companies rallied, adding over ₹1 lakh crore in market valuation. The total market capitalization of the 10 listed group firms reached ₹17.23 lakh crore, the highest since the Hindenburg Research report was released in January 2023.

articles.headline: FMCG sector shows signs of rural demand recovery in Q4
articles.summary: Quarterly earnings from major consumer goods companies indicate a gradual recovery in rural markets. Executives cited stabilizing inflation and a positive monsoon forecast as key drivers for volume growth expectations in the next fiscal year.

articles.headline: US weekly jobless claims fall amid steady labor market cooling
articles.summary: The number of Americans filing new claims for unemployment benefits fell by 8,000 to 215,000. While the labor market remains relatively tight, the underlying trend suggests a gradual cooling that may assist the Federal Reserve in managing inflation.

articles.headline: Three new criminal laws to be implemented from July 1
articles.summary: The Indian government confirmed that the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, and Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam will take effect from July 1, 2024. These laws replace the colonial-era IPC, CrPC, and Evidence Act.

articles.headline: India peak power demand hits record high amid severe heatwave
articles.summary: India's peak power demand reached a record 239 GW as a severe heatwave gripped large parts of the country. The Ministry of Power has directed all generating stations to maintain high availability to meet surging cooling requirements.

articles.headline: IMD issues red alert for severe heatwave in North India
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department has issued a red alert for Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana, and Delhi. Temperatures are expected to remain 4-6 degrees Celsius above normal, with some areas likely to cross 48 degrees Celsius.

articles.headline: Government issues advisory on heatstroke prevention during severe heatwave
articles.summary: The Ministry of Health has released a comprehensive advisory for citizens to prevent heat-related illnesses. The guidelines recommend staying hydrated, avoiding peak sun hours, and recognizing early symptoms of heat exhaustion.

articles.headline: Nvidia shares cross $1,000 mark after stellar Q1 earnings report
articles.summary: Nvidia's stock price surged over 9% in US trading, crossing the $1,000 threshold for the first time. The AI chipmaker reported a 262% jump in quarterly revenue, signaling sustained global demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure.

articles.headline: ISRO successfully conducts 3D-printed liquid engine test for rockets
articles.summary: The Indian Space Research Organisation successfully test-fired a liquid rocket engine manufactured through 3D printing technology. This achievement is expected to reduce manufacturing time by 60% and lead to cost savings in future launches.

articles.headline: State education boards begin announcing Class 10 and 12 results
articles.summary: Several state education boards across India have commenced the declaration of Class 10 and 12 examination results. Early trends indicate a slight increase in overall pass percentages, particularly among girl students, compared to last year.

articles.headline: Sunrisers Hyderabad to face Rajasthan Royals in IPL Qualifier 2
articles.summary: Sunrisers Hyderabad and Rajasthan Royals will compete in the second qualifier of IPL 2024 for a spot in the final against Kolkata Knight Riders. The match is scheduled to be held in Chennai following Rajasthan's win in the eliminator.

articles.headline: AI Stocks Push Taiwan and South Korea Markets Near India’s Valuation
articles.summary: High demand for AI-related stocks like TSMC and Samsung has driven the market capitalizations of Taiwan ($4.6 trillion) and South Korea ($4.2 trillion) close to India’s $4.9 trillion. While India lacks major AI plays, global investors are increasingly focusing on AI-hot markets, highlighting a strategic gap for Indian bourses despite domestic wealth creation.

articles.headline: Indian Music Labels Acquire Regional Catalogues to Boost Digital Revenue
articles.summary: Major music labels including Saregama and Warner Music are acquiring regional catalogues to counter slow paid subscription growth. Consolidation helps big players pool resources and increase bargaining power with streaming platforms. Despite rising investment costs for film music, labels prioritize IP control and predictable content flow as streaming pay rates decline.

articles.headline: Women Hold Minority Share of Demat Accounts Across Indian States
articles.summary: Women represent only 13% to 27% of retail demat account holders in India as of October 2025. According to the statistics ministry’s report, Goa, Sikkim, and Mizoram show the highest female participation, while Bihar, Jammu and Kashmir, and Tripura have the lowest. Participation in major states like Maharashtra and Gujarat remains at 22.9% and 21.1% respectively.

articles.headline: India's Growth Faces Jobs Crisis as Manufacturing Share Stutters
articles.summary: India's shift from agriculture to services has bypassed manufacturing, leaving a low-skilled workforce with stagnant real wages. Average monthly earnings remain below ₹20,000 in eight states. While GDP share has doubled since 2000, manufacturing value added remains below peers like Vietnam. Most workers lack formal contracts, social security, and bargaining power.

articles.headline: Indian Stocks Surge on Hopes of US-Iran Nuclear Negotiation Framework
articles.summary: The Sensex and Nifty rose 1.2% each following reports that Washington and Tehran are working on a memorandum for nuclear negotiations. Brent crude fell to a two-week low, aiding the rupee. While valuations have become more palatable with Nifty's forward P/E at 19x, investors remains cautious about geopolitical volatility and earnings visibility in the near term.

articles.headline: Lenders Scrutinize Financing Agreements Under Material Adverse Change Clauses
articles.summary: Amid the West Asia conflict, law firms report increased scrutiny of Material Adverse Change (MAC) clauses in financing deals. Unlike force majeure, MAC clauses allow lenders to renegotiate or exit deals if a borrower's financial condition deteriorates. Scrutiny is high in energy, aviation, and shipping sectors facing rising input costs and supply-chain disruptions.

articles.headline: Ola Consumer Board Approves IPO Plan Amid Dwindling Cash Reserves
articles.summary: Ola Consumer has commenced IPO preparations as its market share fell to 20-25% at the end of 2025. The company’s net worth dropped 57% to ₹1,490 crore in FY25, while operating losses doubled to ₹662 crore. Experts suggest a public listing is crucial for the company to raise capital to compete with rivals like Uber and Rapido, who hold stronger cash positions.

articles.headline: Bajaj Auto Warns of Demand Softening Despite Strong FY26 Earnings
articles.summary: Bajaj Auto reported a 7% rise in FY26 net profit to ₹9,212 crore, but warned that demand may soften due to rising raw material costs linked to the West Asia conflict. The company noted that price hikes have eroded gains from recent GST cuts. Bajaj also announced a ₹5,633-crore share buyback and an over 10% growth in overall volumes for the fiscal year.

articles.headline: Airlines Limit Portable Power Bank Usage Due to Fire Risks
articles.summary: Airlines including American, Delta, and Southwest are cracking down on portable chargers following a rise in cabin fires. Power banks are the leading cause of lithium-battery thermal runaway incidents on planes. New rules include prohibiting in-flight charging and limiting passengers to one charger. The FAA is considering stricter guidance as incidents have doubled since 2020.

articles.headline: India Taps Alternate Trade Routes to Bypass Strait of Hormuz Blockade
articles.summary: The Indian government is operationalizing alternate shipping services through West Asian ports like Sohar and Fujairah to facilitate cargo movement during the US-Iran war. The Strait of Hormuz, handling 20% of global oil trade, remains largely closed. Officials aim to minimize trade interruptions for energy imports and non-oil exports through the Wana region.

articles.headline: Market Concerns Rise Over Frequent Sebi Policy Reversals and Tweaks
articles.summary: A series of policy reversals by Sebi between January and April has sparked concerns about regulatory uncertainty. The regulator deferred or reversed decisions on solution-oriented mutual funds, intraday borrowing, and nomination rules following industry feedback. Experts suggest some regulations are being framed without adequate consultation, leading to high compliance costs.

articles.headline: Government Foodgrain Stocks Reach Nearly Three Times Mandatory Buffer Norms
articles.summary: Rice and wheat reserves in Food Corporation of India godowns rose to 60.40 million tonnes as of 1 April. This is significantly higher than the mandatory buffer requirement of 21.04 million tonnes. Rice stocks stood at 38.61 million tonnes, while wheat reserves reached 21.79 million tonnes, ensuring sufficient supply despite production concerns.

articles.headline: New Zealand to Introduce Citizenship Test for Migrants From 2027
articles.summary: New Zealand will require migrants seeking citizenship to pass a 20-question multi-choice test in English starting in 2027. The test will cover topics including government and human rights. Candidates must answer at least 15 questions correctly to pass. The move aims to ensure new citizens have a foundational understanding of the country's values and systems.

articles.headline: India and Vietnam Strengthen AI and Defence Ties During Lam Visit
articles.summary: Prime Minister Modi and Vietnamese President To Lam agreed to boost bilateral trade to $25 billion by 2030. Agreements were signed covering rare earths, payment systems, and energy. The two nations aim to elevate defence and security cooperation to ensure regional stability. India will also provide increased market access for Vietnamese agricultural products like durian.

articles.headline: India Maintains Sugar Export Policy as Stable Prices Offset Lower Output
articles.summary: The Indian government currently sees no need to curb sugar exports further as stable prices suggest weaker demand has balanced a production shortfall. Despite betting that output would exceed demand, yields in major growing regions have weakened. India remains the world's second-largest sugar exporter, and current exports are capped at 1.59 million tonnes.

articles.headline: Jaishankar Emphasizes Family Bond as India and Suriname Mark 50 Years
articles.summary: External affairs minister S. Jaishankar described Suriname as "family" during his visit to mark 50 years of diplomatic relations. Bilateral ties have expanded to include infrastructure projects, trade, and cultural links, often supported by Indian Lines of Credit. The visit underscores India's commitment to deepening engagement with the Surinamese diaspora and the wider region.

articles.headline: Subsidy Outgo Reaches 91% of FY26 Target in Eleven Months
articles.summary: Government spending on major subsidies reached ₹3.89 trillion during the April-February period of FY26. Urea subsidies breached the annual target, reaching 101% of revised estimates. While food subsidies stood at 85% of the target, petroleum subsidies remained contained. Analysts suggest war-induced price pressures could force further upward revisions in the next fiscal year.

articles.headline: Larsen & Toubro Reports Revenue Loss Due to West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: L&T missed its FY26 revenue growth target after losing approximately ₹5,000 crore in the March quarter due to geopolitical tensions in West Asia. The region accounts for 78% of the firm's international order book. While the total order book reached a record ₹7.4 trillion, management has tempered growth guidance for FY27 to 10-12% as execution challenges persist.

articles.headline: Mahindra Focuses on SUV Dominance and EV Scaling for Growth
articles.summary: Mahindra & Mahindra reported a 53% jump in Q4 net profit to ₹3,740 crore, driven by strong SUV demand and higher realizations. The company holds a 25.3% revenue market share in the SUV segment. Its EV business reached a 5.1% Ebit margin, a significant improvement. M&M targets a 15-20% EPS growth over the next five years through premium launches and global rationalization.

articles.headline: NSE Confident of OFS Support for IPO at ₹5 Trillion Valuation
articles.summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) stated it has sufficient shareholder participation for its planned IPO, even at a valuation exceeding ₹5 trillion. Current unlisted prices value the exchange at ₹5.06 trillion. While FY26 profit fell 15% to ₹10,302 crore due to lower transaction charges, the IPO remains one of the most anticipated listings in the Indian market for 2026.

articles.headline: United Breweries Margins Squeezed by Rising Packaging and Logistics Costs
articles.summary: United Breweries Ltd reported a decline in FY26 revenue to ₹1,746.3 crore as rising input costs for glass and aluminium cans pressured margins. While beer volumes grew 4% in the final quarter, profit for the year fell to ₹413.4 crore. The Heineken-controlled firm expects cost pressures to persist through the first half of FY27 due to West Asia-related supply disruptions.

articles.headline: Extended West Asia Conflict Could Hit Indian Earnings and Fiscal Deficit
articles.summary: Kotak Institutional Equities' Sanjeev Prasad warns that if the Iran war continues past May, crude oil could average $100 per barrel, pushing India's fiscal deficit to 5%. While 40% of Nifty 50 profits come from global sectors, domestic consumption plays like paints and cement would see margins squeezed. The impact on credit growth and inflation remains a key concern for the fiscal year.

articles.headline: Rivian Explores In-House Lidar Production for Future Self-Driving Vehicles
articles.summary: Electric vehicle maker Rivian is considering developing its own lidar sensors, potentially in partnership with a Chinese firm, to enhance its self-driving technology. CEO RJ Scaringe stated that R2 vehicles launching later this year would include these sensors. The move aims to compete with Tesla’s proprietary technology by providing a 3D view of the road.

articles.headline: Blue Owl's Stack Infrastructure Weighs $30 Billion Sale of Asian Assets
articles.summary: Data centre firm Stack Infrastructure is exploring a full or partial sale of its operations in Australia, Japan, and Malaysia. The deal, which could be valued at over $30 billion, comes as parent company Blue Owl Capital looks to optimize its portfolio. Prospective advisers have been contacted to manage the sale of these critical infrastructure assets in the Asia-Pacific region.

articles.headline: German Giant Bayer to Acquire Glaucoma Drugmaker for $2.45 Billion
articles.summary: Bayer has agreed to buy San Francisco-based Perfuse Therapeutics in a deal worth up to $2.45 billion. The acquisition includes an experimental drug, PER-001, designed to treat glaucoma and diabetes-related eye damage. Currently in clinical trials, the drug fits Bayer’s strategy to expand its pharmaceutical pipeline in the ophthalmology and chronic disease segments.

articles.headline: Chennai-based Veritas Finance Explores ₹1,000 Crore Secondary Transaction
articles.summary: Non-banking financial company Veritas Finance is planning a private transaction worth ₹800-1,000 crore to allow early investors like Lok Capital and BII to exit. The move follows delays in its ₹2,800 crore IPO due to market volatility. The NBFC, which focuses on MSME lending, reported a profit of ₹295 crore in FY25 and serves over 500 branches across 11 states.

articles.headline: VC Firm B Capital Targets Annual $100 Million India Investment
articles.summary: B Capital, co-founded by Eduardo Saverin, plans to deploy $50-100 million annually in India, focusing on AI, deeptech, and energy transition. The firm sees India as its primary market after the US. Key interests include industrial robotics and AI-enabled services. B Capital standard checks range from $5 million to $20 million, with a focus on Series A through Series C rounds.

articles.headline: AI Features Drive User Growth at Meesho Despite Widening Ebitda Loss
articles.summary: E-commerce firm Meesho’s annual transacting user base grew 32% to 264 million in FY26, aided by AI tools like voice search. However, marketplace Ebitda loss widened to ₹1,178 crore due to aggressive investments in technology and customer acquisition. The company is transitioning to autonomous software development where AI agents handle coding and testing to improve reliability.

articles.headline: Home Services Startup Pronto Raises $45 Million at Doubled Valuation
articles.summary: Pronto has closed a $45 million Series B round, valuing the company at $200 million. The funding was led by Epiq Capital and included a $20 million cheque from Lachy Groom. Operating in 190 micromarkets, Pronto averages 26,000 bookings daily. The capital will be used to expand its partner network to overcome supply constraints in India's growing $60 billion home services market.

articles.headline: Marico Targets ₹20,000 Crore Revenue by FY30 Through Premiumization
articles.summary: FMCG major Marico aims to grow its revenue to ₹20,000 crore by FY30 by focusing on wellness, packaged foods, and digital-first brands. CEO Saugata Gupta noted that the foods business is expected to expand 15 times. Despite price hikes of 7% due to input cost volatility, Marico's India volumes grew 8% in FY26, its highest in seven years, as consumers upgraded to branded products.

articles.headline: Legacy Fashion Brands Adopt Hub Models to Meet Quick-Commerce Demands
articles.summary: Fashion retailers like Biba, Libas, and Rare Rabbit are reworking logistics to enable 90-minute deliveries. Instead of converting small stores into warehouses, brands are creating city-level fulfillment hubs to protect the in-store customer experience. The shift follows a surge in funding for quick-fashion startups that offer rapid delivery of party and occasion wear.

articles.headline: Air India Delays Bengaluru MRO Facility Commissioning to 2027
articles.summary: Air India has pushed back the launch of its maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) facility at Bengaluru airport by one year to 2027. The delay is attributed to difficulties in sourcing high-grade steel and specialized bolts from domestic manufacturers for the complex unsupported-roof hangar. The facility is key to reducing the airline's reliance on expensive overseas repairs.

articles.headline: Piramal Finance Automates Entry-Level Hiring Using AI Voice Bots
articles.summary: Piramal Finance hired 262 sales executives in the March quarter using an AI-led process with no human interviews. The voice bot assesses candidates in multiple languages against a standard rubric. MD Jairam Sridharan stated the model reduces human bias and provides more consistent evaluations. While successful for junior roles, human intervention remains necessary for senior hiring.

articles.headline: Aadhar Housing Finance Targets ₹50,000 Crore AUM in Three Years
articles.summary: Blackstone-backed Aadhar Housing Finance expects over 20% growth in assets under management for FY27. The company focuses on low-income borrowers and affordable housing projects. Management stated that West Asia tensions have no material impact as NRI borrowers represent less than 1% of the portfolio. The firm has a long-term goal of reaching ₹1 trillion in AUM.

articles.headline: Godrej Properties Plans ₹39,000 Crore Sales Driven by Luxury Launches
articles.summary: Godrej Properties is targeting record sales this year, backed by a ₹48,000 crore project pipeline. The developer is focusing on premium and luxury projects in Gurugram, Delhi, and Mumbai, including apartments priced over ₹15 crore. Chairperson Pirojsha Godrej noted that the shift mirrors rising demand for higher-value homes among Indian consumers across major metropolitan regions.

articles.headline: L&T Outlines Lakshya 31 Strategy to Double Revenue by FY31
articles.summary: Larsen & Toubro’s new five-year plan, Lakshya 31, aims to double annual revenue to ₹5.8 trillion through diversification into green hydrogen, semiconductors, and data centres. The firm has earmarked ₹42,400 crore for these new ventures. While L&T targets a 16-17% RoE, some analysts expressed concern that owning capital-heavy assets may initially dilute return ratios compared to its asset-light model.

articles.headline: Trump Threatens Iran With Bombing Over Strait of Hormuz Blockade
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump warned Iran that "the bombing starts" if it fails to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and accept a reported peace agreement. The threat came after China's foreign minister called for a ceasefire in the two-month conflict. The blockade has sent fuel prices skyrocketing and disrupted global oil and gas shipments, placing severe pressure on the global economy.

articles.headline: Chinese Firms Defy Sanctions to Supply Drone Components to Iran
articles.summary: Small, obscure Chinese companies are openly shipping dual-use goods like drone engines and fiber-optic cables to Iran and Russia, bypassing U.S. controls. Firms like Xiamen Victory Technology use AI to market German-designed engines used in Shahed attack drones. Because these firms rarely deal in dollars, they have little fear of U.S. sanctions, complicating nonproliferation efforts.

articles.headline: AI Adoption Forces CEOs to Weigh Layoffs Against Increased Productivity
articles.summary: Corporate leaders are divided on whether to use AI for cost-cutting or growth. Coinbase and PayPal have announced significant workforce reductions as AI automates tasks, while Axon Enterprise assures staff that technology will let teams solve more problems without layoffs. Meta is investing heavily in data centers while cutting 8,000 roles to reposition for AI-driven operations.

articles.headline: New Emergency Credit Scheme Unlocks ₹2.55 Trillion for Indian Businesses
articles.summary: The Union cabinet has allocated ₹18,100 crore for ECLGS 5.0 to help MSMEs and airlines manage liquidity stress caused by the West Asia war. The sovereign-guaranteed loans carry zero credit risk for banks and are expected to support loan growth while containing slippages. Incremental funding is capped at 20% of peak working capital utilization for eligible borrowers.

articles.headline: India’s Offshore Tech Hub Revenue Approaches $100 Billion Mark
articles.summary: India’s Global Capability Centres (GCCs) are projected to generate $98.4 billion in revenue for FY26. The sector is moving beyond back-office support to high-value R&D and AI development for global firms like Nvidia and JPMorgan. A large AI-ready workforce and supportive tax policies have accelerated expansion, bringing revenue levels close to original 2030 projections.

articles.headline: RBI Restricts Top-Tier Forex Dealer Licences to Banking Institutions
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has rejected an industry demand to allow non-banks to operate as Authorised Dealer Category-I forex dealers. While the scope for Category-II entities has been expanded, full capital and current account convertibility privileges remain exclusive to banks. The decision maintains a clear regulatory boundary between banking and non-banking financial firms.

articles.headline: Shree Cement Slows Expansion Plans Following Rival Adani's Move
articles.summary: Shree Cement Ltd has dialled back its capacity expansion targets, signaling a cooling in the industry's aggressive capex cycle. CFO Subhash Jajoo stated the company will now focus on rider utilization of existing capacity rather than rapid additions. This follows a similar shift by Ambuja Cements, which moved its 155 million tonne target from FY28 to FY30.

articles.headline: Silver Prices Soar Over ₹10,000 on MCX Tracking Global Cues
articles.summary: Silver futures for July delivery jumped 4.24% to ₹2.54 lakh per kg on the Multi Commodity Exchange. The surge was driven by a weakening US dollar and hopes for progress in US-Iran negotiations, which reduced inflation concerns. Global Comex silver rose 6% to $78 per ounce as investors sought haven assets amid geopolitical volatility and yen intervention expectations.

articles.headline: Brokers Body ANMI Meets RBI Over Proprietary Trade Funding Curbs
articles.summary: Representatives from the Association of NSE Members of India met central bank officials to seek relaxation on proprietary trade funding restrictions. The RBI had deferred a circular limiting bank exposure to capital markets until July. Brokers argue that not all proprietary trades are speculative and seek more flexibility in managing liquidity for non-speculative transactions.

articles.headline: G7 Trade Ministers Target Critical Mineral Supply Chains Dominated by China
articles.summary: Meeting in Paris, G7 ministers sought to secure critical mineral supplies to prevent economic weaponization by China. However, unity is tested by fresh US threats to raise tariffs on European-made cars. China currently dominates markets for minerals used in EVs and defence systems, allowing it to set low prices that can drive competitors out of the market.

articles.headline: India to Host International Big Cat Alliance Summit in New Delhi
articles.summary: India will host the first-ever IBCA summit on 1 June, bringing together leaders from 25 member countries. The summit focuses on conserving seven big cat species, including tigers, lions, and cheetahs. Over 400 stakeholders will participate in the event, themed "Save Big Cats, Save Humanity, Save Ecosystem," under an intergovernmental alliance launched by PM Modi in 2023.

articles.headline: Sun Pharma’s $11.75 Billion Organon Deal Shifts Focus to Emerging Markets
articles.summary: Sun Pharmaceutical’s acquisition of Organon & Co. will double its revenue to $12.4 billion and mark a strategic shift away from US generics. The deal provides Sun with a dominant presence in China and a top-7 global biosimilars platform. CEO Aalok Shanghvi, representing the next generation, will lead the combined international business, which now accounts for over 50% of total revenue.

articles.headline: Sweep-in Fixed Deposits Offer Better Yields for Emergency Cash
articles.summary: Financial advisors recommend using sweep-in facilities to park emergency funds, as idle savings accounts often earn only 2-4%, failing to beat inflation. Sweep-in FDs automatically move surplus cash above a threshold into higher-interest deposits while maintaining liquidity. However, users should be aware of LIFO vs FIFO methods and potential premature withdrawal penalties of 0.5-1%.

articles.headline: India’s Semiconductor Strategy Faces Test of Evolving AI Demand Patterns
articles.summary: India is building 12 semiconductor plants and pursuing design alliances for 2nm chips, but must navigate a global shift from general computing to specialized AI infrastructure. While Indian fabs currently focus on 28-120nm nodes for automotive and telecom, cutting-edge AI requires more advanced processing power and a robust data centre ecosystem to be truly competitive.

articles.headline: Global Energy Markets Face Long Normalization Despite Potential War End
articles.summary: Even if the West Asia war ends soon, experts warn it will take months to restore normalcy to oil and gas supplies. Oil consumption is concentrated in sectors like road freight and shipping with few substitutes. Countries like Vietnam and Sri Lanka have already introduced fuel rationing. Governments are urged to invest more in solar energy and work-from-home schedules to manage shortages.

articles.headline: Proposed Securities Market Code Seeks to Clarify Ambiguous Penalty Rules
articles.summary: The proposed Securities Market Code 2025 aims to resolve the tension between mandatory minimum penalties and adjudicatory discretion in India's legal framework. Current inconsistencies under the Sebi Act create uncertainty for market participants. The Code is an opportunity to decide if minimum penalties are absolute floors or if departures are permitted based on harm and gain factors.

articles.headline: SME Exporters Face Confidence Gap Amid Rising Geopolitical Uncertainty
articles.summary: A survey of 461 SME exporters reveals a widening gap between their optimism for growth and the deteriorating risk environment. The Risk Momentum Index signals worsening conditions for tariffs and supply chains. Over 52% of firms plan to scale back international exposure, while many are unaware of benefits from free trade agreements, highlighting a failure in policy transmission.

articles.headline: Berkshire Successor Greg Abel Faces Pressure to Match Buffett’s Performance
articles.summary: Greg Abel, the new CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, faces investor skepticism as the company’s stock underperforms the S&P 500 by over 40 percentage points. Berkshire is holding a record $397 billion in cash, missing out on the AI-driven market rally. Analysts doubt investors will show the same patience for Abel’s disciplined, long-term approach as they did for Warren Buffett.

articles.headline: Vadodara’s Art Ecosystem Supports Emerging Students Through Industry Shows
articles.summary: The annual degree show at Maharaja Sayajirao University in Vadodara has become a vital platform for visual arts students to get noticed by galleries and curators. Local infrastructure like Gallery White and the Ark Foundation for the Arts provide mentorship and studio space. The city's lower cost of living and strong community sense encourage graduates to stay and experiment.

articles.headline: Afrobeats Star Tiwa Savage Launches Lagos Music Training Foundation
articles.summary: Nigerian singer Tiwa Savage has partnered with Berklee College of Music to launch a foundation for aspiring African artists. The program selected 100 students for workshops on industry workings and vocal coaching. Savage aims to professionalize the Afrobeats ecosystem and provide access to elite music education, offering $2.1 million in scholarships to the first cohort.

articles.headline: Travel Excerpt: Navigating Archaic Visa Processes for 2019 Iran Trip
articles.summary: An excerpt from Shivya Nath’s 'Rootless and Restless' describes the circuitous route to obtaining an Iranian visa in 2019, including medical tests and separate physical documents. The travelogue highlights the challenges of navigating a country where international credit cards and major booking websites do not function, emphasizing the beauty of slow travel through public transport.

articles.headline: Banks seek promoters’ skin in the game for Vi funding
articles.summary: Lenders are seeking guarantees from group companies or capital infusion backstops from Vodafone Idea (Vi) promoters before approving a ₹35,000 crore loan request. Banks want broader participation across the system, including private lenders. While the government holds a 49% stake, promoters own 25.64%. No definitive funding commitment has been made yet as discussions continue.

articles.headline: Banks expect MSME credit spurt from ECLGS 5.0
articles.summary: The Centre's Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme (ECLGS) 5.0 is expected to drive ₹12,000-15,000 crore in additional bank lending to MSMEs. The scheme aims to support businesses facing supply chain disruptions from the West Asia conflict. It offers 100% guarantee coverage for MSMEs and includes a special ₹5,000 crore facility for the airline sector to maintain operations.

articles.headline: Unincorporated sector added 7.5 mn jobs in 2025
articles.summary: India’s unincorporated non-agricultural sector added 7.5 million workers in 2025, reaching a total of 128.1 million. According to NSO data, Uttar Pradesh holds the largest share of workers at 14.5%. Digital adoption surged, with internet use for business rising to 39%. Notably, 27% of these establishments are headed by female proprietors, particularly in the manufacturing sector.

articles.headline: White-collar crimes rose to 6% of IPC cases in 2024
articles.summary: Economic offenses and cybercrimes increased to 6% of all Indian Penal Code cases in 2024, despite an overall 6% decline in total crime. NCRB data shows fraud cases surged by 191% in a single year. Telangana, Rajasthan, and Maharashtra reported the highest number of economic offenses. Cybercrime's share of total cases rose to 2.9%, well above pre-pandemic levels.

articles.headline: TSMC widens weight gap with India in MSCI EM index
articles.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) has increased its weighting in the MSCI Emerging Markets index to 14.2%, outpacing India’s 11.94%. Driven by the AI boom, Taiwan has overtaken China as the top weight in the index. India's weighting has reached its lowest level in over six years as global passive capital flows increasingly favor the chipmaking leader.

articles.headline: Foreign university campuses to drive competition for Indian academic talent
articles.summary: Foreign universities establishing campuses in India are expected to create up to 15,000 faculty roles by 2035. This shift is likely to drive up salaries, as these institutions offer 20-30% premiums over top public institutes like IITs. India currently faces a faculty shortfall of 600,000. Demand is expected to be highest in specialized fields like data science and AI.

articles.headline: India’s GCC revenue close to $100 billion in FY26
articles.summary: India's Global Capability Centres (GCCs) recorded estimated revenue of $98.4 billion in FY26, with the number of centers crossing 2,117. Employing 2.36 million people, these hubs are shifting from delivery centers to value-driven AI centers of excellence. Bengaluru and Hyderabad remain the top hubs. Professionals with niche skills now make up 27% of the total GCC workforce.

articles.headline: Signs of US-Iran deal cool oil prices and lift markets
articles.summary: Reports of a potential peace memorandum between the US and Iran to end the Gulf war triggered a sharp rally in Indian markets. Brent crude fell 7% to near $102 a barrel, easing inflation concerns. The Sensex jumped 941 points, while the rupee strengthened to 94.61 against the dollar. Investor optimism was fueled by hopes that the Strait of Hormuz would soon reopen for trade.

articles.headline: DeepSeek could be valued at $50 bn in maiden fundraise
articles.summary: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek is in talks for a funding round that could value the company at $50 billion. China's national AI fund and Tencent are reportedly in discussions to lead the round, which seeks to raise $3-4 billion. This marks a strategic reversal for the large language model builder, which had previously avoided outside funding while competing with ByteDance.

articles.headline: L&T bags largest ever domestic metals order from JSW Steel
articles.summary: Larsen & Toubro secured a 'mega' order from JSW Steel valued between ₹10,000 crore and ₹15,000 crore. This is the largest domestic order for L&T’s metals business. The project involves engineering, procurement, and installation of process facilities, including blast furnaces and steel melt shops. It marks a significant milestone in the domestic industrial infrastructure sector.

articles.headline: VFS Global-led group to launch 60 model sub-registrar offices
articles.summary: A consortium led by VFS Global won a five-year contract from the Maharashtra government to launch 60 model sub-registrar offices. The initiative aims to digitally transform property registration into a citizen-centric system. The rollout will occur in phases, focusing on modernizing infrastructure and improving efficiency for property-related transactions across the state.

articles.headline: M&M strengthens supply chains amid world geopolitical crises
articles.summary: Mahindra & Mahindra is refining its supply-chain strategy to counter geopolitical volatility. CEO Anish Shah detailed a risk-mapping exercise for ₹1 trillion in purchases and 100,000 parts. Measures include increasing inventory buffers, localizing suppliers, and creating an intelligence desk. The move aims for structural resilience in managing semiconductor and rare earth supplies.

articles.headline: Two-wheeler firms expect high single-digit growth in FY27
articles.summary: India's two-wheeler industry expects high single-digit volume growth in FY27, driven by strong motorcycle and scooter demand. Hero MotoCorp CEO Harshavardhan Chitale noted that input costs haven't fully slowed demand. The firm is ramping up electric vehicle capacity and plans to launch vehicles compatible with higher ethanol blends to meet low-emission goals.

articles.headline: Adani files caveat in SC amid likely Vedanta appeal
articles.summary: Adani Enterprises filed a caveat in the Supreme Court to ensure it is heard in any appeal by Vedanta Ltd regarding the insolvency of Jaiprakash Associates Ltd (JAL). This follows an NCLAT ruling that upheld Adani’s resolution plan for JAL. A caveat prevents any interim order from being passed without notifying the interested party in the ongoing dispute.

articles.headline: India needs comprehensive energy storage policy to steer through shocks
articles.summary: S&P Global warns India needs a comprehensive energy storage policy to manage shocks from the West Asia conflict. India relies on the Gulf for 90% of crude and 50% of gas but has less than 2 Gw of battery storage and limited strategic reserves. S&P lowered India's FY27 GDP forecast to 6.6%, citing rising freight costs and input pressure across manufacturing and services.

articles.headline: Seci floats tender for 500,000 tonnes of green methanol
articles.summary: The Solar Energy Corporation of India launched a tender for 500,000 tonnes of green methanol per annum under the National Green Hydrogen Mission. The project, part of the SIGHT programme, aims to decarbonize shipping and industry. With a total outlay of ₹19,744 crore, the mission provides financial incentives for companies setting up domestic production facilities.

articles.headline: RIL plans Jamnagar refinery maintenance shutdown starting late May
articles.summary: Reliance Industries is expected to begin a maintenance shutdown at its Jamnagar refinery complex in late May. The government is coordinating schedules to ensure all domestic refineries do not close simultaneously, maintaining fuel availability during the West Asia crisis. The shutdown will follow the resumption of operations at Nayara Energy’s Vadinar site.

articles.headline: Most manufacturers saw stable output in Q4 despite crisis
articles.summary: A Ficci survey shows 93% of Indian manufacturers reported higher or stable production in Q4FY26, up from 91%. Despite rising input costs from the West Asia crisis, domestic demand remains steady. Hiring intentions also improved marginally. However, 70% of firms noted production costs as a percentage of sales increased due to higher raw material and electricity prices.

articles.headline: EAC-PM bats for extending paternity leave to private sector
articles.summary: The Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister recommended phased reforms to extend statutory paternity leave to private sector employees. Currently, only public sector and central government workers have statutory leave. The council suggests a gender-neutral parental leave framework and co-funded insurance products to redistribute caregiving and boost female workforce participation.

articles.headline: Council pitches for changes in priority sector lending norms
articles.summary: The Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister suggested shifting priority sector lending (PSL) focus from economic to social equity. A working paper argues that diverting credit to inefficient sectors harms total factor productivity. It recommends removing outdated categories from PSL definitions and focusing strictly on small farmers and weaker sections.

articles.headline: Coal India saw muted April e-auction demand despite supply
articles.summary: Coal India allocated only 39% of the 30.5 million tonnes offered in its April e-auctions, indicating a slow start to FY27. Despite the low volume, pricing was resilient with average premiums at 51% over notified prices. The subdued demand suggests cautious buying by consumers as the state-run miner maintains adequate supply through single-window agnostic routes.

articles.headline: Mandaviya to launch free health check-up initiative for workers
articles.summary: Union Minister Mansukh Mandaviya will launch a nationwide 'Annual Health Check-Up Initiative' for workers aged 40 and above on May 7. The program, mandated under new Labour Codes, will start at ESIC Medical College, New Delhi, and expand to 11 other ESIC hospitals. The initiative aims to provide regular health screenings to improve worker welfare across various states.

articles.headline: South Indian Bank Q4 profit jumped 19% to ₹408 crore
articles.summary: South Indian Bank reported a 19.3% rise in net profit for Q4FY26, reaching ₹408 crore. The increase was driven by an 84.8% decline in provisions to ₹34 crore. Net interest income grew 5.4% to ₹915 crore, while the net interest margin stood at 2.95%. The bank benefited from a steep drop in provisions compared to ₹224 crore in the year-ago period.

articles.headline: KPIT Tech profit dips 33% after uptick in costs
articles.summary: KPIT Technologies reported a 33.3% decline in consolidated net profit to ₹163 crore for Q4FY26. The dip was primarily due to higher finance costs from acquisition loans and a ₹31.16 crore foreign exchange loss. Despite the profit decline, the Pune-based automotive solutions firm continues its acquisition-led growth strategy, though currency fluctuations impacted margins.

articles.headline: GCPL profit rises 9.6% on volume growth and cost management
articles.summary: FMCG major Godrej Consumer Products reported a 9.67% increase in consolidated net profit to ₹451.77 crore for the March quarter. Growth was led by 6% domestic volume expansion and efficient cost management. Revenue rose 11% to ₹3,900.44 crore. The company maintained an operating margin of 21.7% as Ebitda grew 10% during the quarter.

articles.headline: Polycab India net profit rises 6% to ₹773 crore
articles.summary: Polycab India reported a 6.3% rise in Q4 net profit to ₹773 crore, beating analyst estimates. Revenue from operations jumped 27% to ₹8,864 crore, driven by strong demand for wires and cables from the infrastructure and housing sectors. The company's largest segment, wires and cables, grew 29% to ₹7,762 crore, while electrical goods rose 39.3%.

articles.headline: Indian Bank plans ₹5,000 crore QIP for ECL transition
articles.summary: Indian Bank plans to raise ₹5,000 crore via QIP in Q3FY27 to absorb the impact of the upcoming Expected Credit Loss (ECL) framework starting April 2027. CEO Binod Kumar estimated the ECL transition impact at ₹4,000-6,000 crore. The bank has already built a ₹800 crore buffer and believes the capital raise will adequately support regulatory compliance.

articles.headline: Railways clears track for South Coast Railway zone
articles.summary: The Centre notified South Coast Railway (SCoR) as India's 18th railway zone, headquartered in Visakhapatnam. Operational from June 1, the 3,300-km zone resolves a long dispute between Andhra Pradesh and Odisha. To balance regional interests, the profitable Waltair division was split, with Odisha sections forming a new Rayagada division within the East Coast Railway.

articles.headline: MBDA to set up missile MRO facility for IAF
articles.summary: European missile maker MBDA signed an agreement with the Indian Air Force to develop a maintenance, repair, and operations (MRO) facility for French MICA missiles. The IAF will operate and maintain the facility, which will support missiles used on Rafale and Mirage 2000-5 fighters. MBDA will provide long-term tools, equipment, and technical support for the project.

articles.headline: AI showing bias against poor, says CJI Surya Kant
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant stated that artificial intelligence is exhibiting inherent bias against the poor. Speaking at the Dinkar Memorial Lecture, he emphasized that social justice remains essential in the age of AI. He noted that reports highlight how technology can discriminate and stressed that the legal fraternity must ensure technology serves human dignity.

articles.headline: RBI halts fresh money changer applications and ends franchisees
articles.summary: The RBI will not consider fresh applications for full-fledged money changers (FFMCs) and ordered existing ones to end franchisee arrangements within two years. Under new 2026 regulations, a three-tier structure replaces the old framework to rationalise foreign exchange delivery. A new AD Category III has been created for entities offering forex as part of underlying business.

articles.headline: Centre to push for PM-SHRI rollout in three states
articles.summary: The Ministry of Education is making a fresh attempt to roll out the flagship PM-SHRI school scheme in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, and Kerala. The ministry will ask chief secretaries to sign MoUs to develop 14,500 model schools aligned with the NEP 2020. Implementation in these opposition-ruled states was previously slow due to objections over adoption of the education policy.

articles.headline: School management committee guidelines launched by Pradhan
articles.summary: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan launched the School Management Committee (SMC) Guidelines 2026 to improve community participation and school infrastructure under NEP 2020. Pradhan noted that new NCERT textbooks are available up to Class 9, with senior secondary books to follow. The guidelines aim to enhance learning outcomes through better local governance in schools.

articles.headline: Uber forecasts strong quarter bookings despite West Asia woes
articles.summary: Uber Technologies forecast second-quarter bookings above estimates, driven by strong ride-hailing and delivery demand in international markets. Uber expects gross bookings between $56.25 billion and $57.75 billion. Despite fuel cost pressures from the West Asia conflict, the company's strategy of price stability and platform expansion for businesses continues to drive growth.

articles.headline: Thailand clears $29 bn investments for data infrastructure
articles.summary: Thailand approved $29 billion in investment projects to become a regional AI and cloud hub. TikTok leads with a $25 billion project to expand data storage and processing. Other approved facilities include data centers by DAMAC Group and Bridge Data Centres. The plan includes digital literacy programs as the country pushes for leadership in Southeast Asia's cloud services market.

articles.headline: Actress Q'orianka Kilcher sues James Cameron over Avatar likeness
articles.summary: Actress Q'orianka Kilcher has sued James Cameron and Disney, alleging her likeness was stolen to create the character Neytiri in 'Avatar.' The suit claims her facial structure was replicated using a teenage photo without permission. Kilcher seeks damages from the film's profits, warning that unauthorized use of biometric identity could become standard in the age of AI.

articles.headline: Israel to transfer jet fuel to Germany amid crisis
articles.summary: Israel will transfer jet fuel to Germany following a request from Berlin due to the Strait of Hormuz crisis. The Israeli energy ministry stated that cargoes will be coordinated with refineries. The move aims to prevent a fuel supply crunch in Europe ahead of the summer travel peak. Israel is also exploring natural gas assistance for Germany to mitigate regional energy disruptions.

articles.headline: Bondada targets 25 Gw RE goal with ₹10,000 crore investment
articles.summary: Bondada Engineering plans to invest ₹10,000 crore over four years to achieve a 25 Gw renewable energy capacity by 2030. The firm is also exploring data centers and defense manufacturing. Of the 25 Gw target, 16 Gw will be solar and 9 Gw battery storage. The company has a ₹7,147 crore order book but is currently facing delays in solar projects due to a lack of grid availability.

articles.headline: Samsung bets on AI and experiential retail for India play
articles.summary: Samsung India is focusing on AI-driven solutions and integrated device ecosystems to grow its enterprise division. The company launched experience studios in Mumbai and Gurugram to showcase digital infrastructure for retail and corporate sectors. Security remains a priority with Samsung's Knox platform being a core offering for secure data and device management in the country.

articles.headline: PhonePe launches AI tool for merchant payment gateway integrations
articles.summary: PhonePe announced an AI integration agent to reduce payment gateway onboarding time for merchants from weeks to minutes. The tool is built for AI coding assistants and uses PhonePe's proprietary intelligence to help merchants understand and generate payment code through a conversational interface. The initiative aims to collapse technical hurdles for merchants going live.

articles.headline: Epic Group plans $150 million technical textiles investment in Odisha
articles.summary: Global apparel major Epic Group will invest $150 million to set up a technical textiles facility in Odisha. This follows a $100 million investment in its first apparel unit in the state. The new facility will focus on high-value synthetic fabrics for activewear and performance apparel for export markets. Land acquisition is currently underway in the Khurda district.

articles.headline: Alpha Corp to invest ₹750 crore in stalled Earth Infra assets
articles.summary: Alpha Corp will invest ₹750 crore to complete three stalled projects of Earth Infrastructure Ltd in Greater Noida and Gurugram. This follows a Supreme Court decision restoring resolution plans for over 3,000 buyers. The acquired assets have a revenue potential of ₹1,200 crore. Construction is expected to finish in four years once principal dues to the Noida authority are settled.

articles.headline: Motilal Oswal AMC to sponsor pension funds under NPS
articles.summary: Motilal Oswal Asset Management has received PFRDA approval to become a sponsor for a pension fund under the National Pension System (NPS). The firm will set up a separate investment manager to oversee NPS contributions. The initiative aims to build sustainable retirement wealth using a research-driven, long-term investment approach for subscribers.

articles.headline: Lawyer in judicial custody for contempt: SC advocates seek intervention
articles.summary: The Supreme Court Bar Association expressed concern after a junior lawyer was sent to 24-hour judicial custody for contempt by the Andhra Pradesh High Court. The SCBA urged the Chief Justice of India to intervene, stressing that judicial power must be used with restraint and compassion, particularly toward young lawyers still finding their footing in the legal profession.

articles.headline: Sahakar Taxi Cooperative enters ride-hailing market as Bharat Taxi
articles.summary: Government-backed Bharat Taxi is using a cooperative model to compete with private ride-hailing apps like Uber and Ola. Registered drivers are stakeholders and pay a fixed daily subscription fee rather than commissions. While it has 517,000 drivers and 5 million customers, the platform faces challenges in ride volume compared to established players in major cities like Delhi-NCR.

articles.headline: Operation Sindoor anniversary: India's strikes shift defense spending strategy
articles.summary: A year after Operation Sindoor strikes on terror bases in Pakistan, India's defense budget has risen to nearly 2% of GDP. Budgetary allocation for FY27 reached ₹7.85 trillion, focusing on air defense, drones, and cognitive warfare. The military is creating four new tri-service organizations for geospatial, data, drone, and cognitive action to modernize national security.

articles.headline: NSE developing GenAI tools for compliance and operational efficiency
articles.summary: The National Stock Exchange is developing in-house generative AI tools for compliance, customer support, and software development. In an analyst call, NSE stated the tools will be on-premises to support tech resilience and handle rising volumes. The exchange's annual technology expenses rose 10% to ₹1,315 crore in FY26 as part of its infrastructure strengthening efforts.

articles.headline: Sebi directs significant index providers to register within six months
articles.summary: Sebi mandated that index providers of 'significant indices'—benchmarks tracked by over ₹20,000 crore in AUM—must register with the regulator. The new rules identify 48 indices across NSE, BSE, and Crisil. Providers must form separate legal entities for index activities within two years. The regulation aims to enhance governance and transparency in index tracking for mutual funds.

articles.headline: Amfi reclassification may reshuffle over 30 stocks in July
articles.summary: Association of Mutual Funds in India (Amfi) is expected to reshuffle over 30 stocks in its July reclassification. Predicted updates to largecap include BSE, Jindal Steel, and Hitachi Energy. Stocks like Indian Hotels and Max Healthcare may slip to midcap. The largecap mcap cutoff is estimated at ₹1.06 trillion, while the midcap cutoff is expected to drop to around ₹32,300 crore.

articles.headline: M&M shares surge on SUV growth and robust product pipeline
articles.summary: Analysts remain bullish on Mahindra & Mahindra following strong Q4 results driven by SUV demand. M&M plans 10 new ICE SUV products and three electric models by 2031. Standalone profit surged 53% year-on-year, though rising commodity costs pose near-term margin risks. Brokerages expect mid-to-high teens growth for the SUV segment as the firm ramps up manufacturing capacity.

articles.headline: Individual home insurance plans offer better fire protection than societies
articles.summary: Experts advise homeowners to opt for individual fire insurance plans, as housing society group policies often provide only basic coverage for structure. Personal plans can cover both structure and contents on a 'new for old' basis. Insurers recommend reinstatement value for rebuilding costs and maintaining detailed inventories of valuables like jewellery and electronics.

articles.headline: Bulls Back on Crude Mood as Oil Prices Drop 8 Percent
articles.summary: Indian equity indices rose over 1% on Wednesday, their strongest single-day gain in three weeks, as oil prices plummeted 8% on hopes of a US-Iran truce. The Sensex closed at 24,812.40 and Nifty at 24,330.90. Investors cheered the possibility of a diplomatic resolution, which eased fears of supply disruptions and global market volatility.

articles.headline: VCs Focus on Early-Stage Deeptech Startups Over Big-Ticket Deals
articles.summary: Venture capitalists are shifting focus to early-stage deals in deeptech sectors like AI, healthcare, and precision manufacturing. Investors are avoiding large, late-stage commitments due to market uncertainty. Startups like Parsec Aerospace and Alteon Energy are among those in talks for seed and Series A funding rounds ranging from $2 million to $6.5 million.

articles.headline: AI Skills Command 30-60 Percent Salary Premium for Indian Techies
articles.summary: AI-skilled professionals in India are earning up to 60% more than peers in other tech fields. Entry-level salaries for AI roles are reaching ₹20 lakh annually, compared to ₹12 lakh for regular IT roles. Recruitment agencies report that 45-50% of organizations are looking to hire talent with AI expertise to drive productivity and innovation.

articles.headline: Operation Sindoor Reshaping Indian Armed Forces One Year Later
articles.summary: A year after 'Operation Sindoor' destroyed a terrorist headquarters in Pakistan, the Indian military is undergoing significant shifts. The operation has prompted moves toward unmanned warfare, long-range precision weapons, and localized production. The focus is now on sensor-to-shooter technology and creating modular, self-contained combat units for faster deployment.

articles.headline: IPL Finals Venue Shifts to Ahmedabad Amid Ticket Row
articles.summary: The BCCI has moved the 2026 IPL final from Bengaluru to the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. The decision follows a controversy over ticket demands from Karnataka MLAs and MPs. While the Karnataka State Cricket Association expressed disappointment, the venue change was finalized to avoid further political friction over seat allocations.

articles.headline: Royal Enfield to Invest ₹2,200 Crore in New Andhra Plant
articles.summary: Royal Enfield is setting up a new manufacturing unit and vendor park in Andhra Pradesh with an investment of ₹2,200 crore. This marks the company's first major expansion outside Tamil Nadu. The project is expected to create 5,000 direct and indirect jobs and will have an ultimate production capacity of 14.6 lakh units per annum.

articles.headline: India Exports Reach Record $863 Billion in FY26
articles.summary: India's total goods and services exports reached a record $863.1 billion in the 2025-26 fiscal year, up 8.7% from the previous year. The growth was primarily driven by the services sector, which touched $421.3 billion. The Ministry of Commerce and Industry attributed the performance to strong demand in international markets despite global geopolitical tensions.

articles.headline: HC Petition Seeks to Halt Tata Trusts Meeting Over Composition
articles.summary: A writ petition filed in the Bombay High Court seeks a stay on a Tata Trusts meeting scheduled for Friday. The petitioner alleges that the current board composition violates statutory limits under the Maharashtra Public Trust Act. The court is asked to intervene on grounds that recent appointments did not follow legal amendments mandated for public trusts.

articles.headline: Ministry Seeks July Approval for ₹20,000 Crore Carbon Capture Scheme
articles.summary: The Union Power Ministry is aiming for Cabinet approval by July for a ₹20,000 crore incentive scheme to promote carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS). The scheme will target hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and thermal power. It aims to help India reach its 2030 emission reduction targets through localized technology deployment.

articles.headline: Companies May Slash IPO Issue Sizes to Combat Market Volatility
articles.summary: At least 10 Indian companies are considering reducing their IPO sizes by up to 50% to ensure successful listings. This move follows market volatility and a cautious appetite from institutional investors. By reducing issue sizes, firms hope to avoid the need to refile draft prospectuses with SEBI while still meeting their immediate funding requirements.

articles.headline: Rupee Records Highest Single-Day Surge in a Month
articles.summary: The Indian rupee appreciated 67 paise to close at 94.61 against the US dollar on Wednesday. This was the steepest single-day gain in over a month, driven by a sharp drop in global oil prices and optimism regarding a US-Iran peace deal. The rally provided relief to the domestic currency, which had recently hit a lifetime low of 95.28.

articles.headline: United Breweries to Exit Unprofitable States Over Margin Pressure
articles.summary: United Breweries (UBL) is considering pulling back from Indian states where high taxes and strict price controls are hurting profitability. The company aims to focus on regions with better margin potential. High input costs for glass and aluminum, combined with fixed retail prices in certain states, have made operations unsustainable in those markets.

articles.headline: Post-Poll Violence Continues in Bengal with Four Deaths Reported
articles.summary: Incidents of violence, including arson and clashes, have been reported across West Bengal following the assembly election results. At least four deaths have occurred in connection with post-poll clashes. The Election Commission has directed strict action against those inciting violence. Over 200 FIRs have been registered, and central forces remain deployed to maintain order.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Questions Law on Appointment of Election Commissioners
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has asked the central government to explain why the Chief Justice of India was excluded from the selection panel for Election Commissioners under the 2023 Act. The court is hearing petitions challenging the constitutionality of the new law, which gives the executive more control over the appointments. The hearing will continue on Thursday.

articles.headline: Puducherry Assembly Dissolved; N Rangasamy to Lead 5th Term
articles.summary: The Puducherry Legislative Assembly has been dissolved by Lieutenant Governor K Kailashnathan following the recent election results. N Rangasamy is set to lead the Union Territory for a fifth term after the AINRC-led NDA won 12 out of 16 seats it contested. The new government is expected to be sworn in shortly to begin its five-year tenure.

articles.headline: Quad Foreign Ministers to Meet in Delhi Following Trump China Visit
articles.summary: Foreign Ministers from India, the US, Japan, and Australia are scheduled to meet in New Delhi. The meeting will focus on critical minerals, emerging technology, and maritime security in the Indo-Pacific. This diplomatic gathering follows President Trump's visit to China and aims to maintain momentum for the Quad's strategic initiatives amidst regional shifts.

articles.headline: Private Hospitals Reduce Participation in Government Health Schemes
articles.summary: Several major private hospital chains are scaling back their participation in government schemes like CGHS and ECHS. The decision is driven by low reimbursement rates and significant payment delays from the government. Hospitals are instead prioritizing high-value private patients to maintain profitability, raising concerns about affordable healthcare access for beneficiaries.

articles.headline: L&T Moderates Growth Targets Despite ₹43,000 Crore Capex Plan
articles.summary: Larsen & Toubro has moderated its long-term 'Lakshya 2031' growth targets due to a more cautious global economic outlook. However, the engineering giant still plans a capital expenditure of ₹43,000 crore for the current year. The firm is focusing on executing its existing massive order book while navigating inflationary pressures and geopolitical risks in the Middle East.

articles.headline: SRF to Invest ₹2,300 Crore in Eco-Friendly Refrigerant Plant
articles.summary: SRF Limited has announced a ₹2,300 crore investment to set up a new manufacturing plant for next-generation, eco-friendly refrigerant gases. These gases have low global-warming potential and are part of the company's commitment to the Montreal Protocol. The plant will help ensure India remains self-reliant in critical cooling technologies as global regulations tighten.

articles.headline: RBI Sets Turnover Thresholds for Non-Bank Money Changers
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has introduced new regulations for non-bank money changers, including a minimum annual turnover threshold of ₹10 crore. The move is aimed at strengthening the oversight of the foreign exchange sector and improving compliance with anti-money laundering norms. Existing firms have two years to meet the new requirements to continue their operations.

articles.headline: Nationwide GPS-Based Highway Tolling by End of Year
articles.summary: Union Minister Nitin Gadkari has announced that India will implement a nationwide, barrier-free GPS-based tolling system by the end of 2026. The new system will eliminate physical toll plazas, with charges calculated based on the exact distance traveled using number plate recognition. The initiative aims to reduce travel time and improve the efficiency of the national highway network.

articles.headline: Freshworks to Lay Off 500 Employees in AI-Led Restructuring
articles.summary: SaaS company Freshworks has announced it will lay off 11% of its global workforce, approximately 500 employees. The restructuring is part of a strategic shift to integrate more AI capabilities into its product offerings and streamline operations. The company expects to record a one-time restructuring charge of $8 million in the April-June quarter as it refocuses on AI growth.

articles.headline: Paytm Reports Net Profit of ₹552 Crore in Fourth Quarter
articles.summary: One97 Communications, the parent company of Paytm, reported a consolidated net profit of ₹552 crore for the fourth quarter ended March 2026. This is a significant turnaround from a loss of ₹663 crore in the same period last year. The company's revenue from operations also grew to ₹8,437 crore, driven by a surge in digital payments and financial services usage.

articles.headline: Trump Warns of Bombing if US-Iran Peace Deal Fails
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has warned that 'bombing starts' if a final peace agreement with Iran is not reached within 48 hours. While a memorandum of understanding has been drafted to end the long-standing conflict, Trump emphasized that a full commitment to the terms is required. Global markets are on edge as the deadline for a diplomatic resolution approaches.

articles.headline: North Korea Defines South as Separate Nation in New Constitution
articles.summary: North Korea has revised its constitution to officially define South Korea as a separate and hostile state, abandoning its long-standing goal of peaceful reunification. The change, led by Kim Jong Un, marks a significant shift in the North's geopolitical stance. The new constitution also solidifies the North's maritime borders and categorizes the South as its primary enemy.

articles.headline: Suspense in Tamil Nadu as TVK majority math yet to add up
articles.summary: Actor-turned-politician C Joseph Vijay met Governor Rajendra Arlekar to stake claim to power after his TVK party won 108 seats in the 234-member assembly. Though supported by five Congress MLAs, he is 10 seats short of a simple majority. Lok Bhavan is seeking legal opinion before inviting him to form the next government following the end of the 50-year-old Dravidian duopoly.

articles.headline: Iran reviews US peace proposal as Trump notes progress in talks
articles.summary: Iran is reviewing a one-page memorandum of understanding proposed by the US to end the war in the Gulf. The deal would unblock shipping in the Strait of Hormuz and lift sanctions, while leaving nuclear program issues for later. US President Donald Trump stated that Tehran is eager to negotiate, though he issued an ultimatum for them to accept the deal or face renewed bombing.

articles.headline: US Navy fires on Iranian oil tanker attempting to break blockade
articles.summary: A US Navy F/A-18 Super Hornet disabled the rudder of the Iranian-flagged oil tanker M/T Hasna after it ignored warnings to stop. The vessel was attempting to break Washington’s blockade of Iranian ports. US CENTCOM confirmed the tanker is no longer transiting to Iran. The incident occurred as Washington and Tehran work toward a memorandum to end hostilities.

articles.headline: Israel strikes Beirut southern suburbs for first time since April ceasefire
articles.summary: Israel conducted air strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs on Wednesday, marking the first such attack since the April 17 ceasefire with Hezbollah. The strike killed Malek Ballout, the operations commander of Hezbollah's elite Radwan force. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed the military targeted the commander, signaling a potential escalation in the region.

articles.headline: Congress MLAs to meet in Thiruvananthapuram to select Kerala Chief Minister
articles.summary: All 63 newly elected Congress MLAs in Kerala will meet on Thursday to initiate the selection of the next Chief Minister. AICC observers Mukul Wasnik and Ajay Maken will attend to gauge preferences. Senior leaders VD Satheesan, KC Venugopal, and Ramesh Chennithala are the primary contenders for the post after the UDF won 102 seats in the 140-member assembly.

articles.headline: Punjab CM Mann accuses BJP of orchestrating Jalandhar and Amritsar blasts
articles.summary: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann alleged that the twin blasts in Jalandhar and Amritsar were part of the BJP's election strategy to create unrest. The BJP dismissed the claims as anti-national, while the state DGP suggested a conspiracy involving Pakistan's ISI. The explosions targeted BSF and Army cantonment areas, using IEDs detonated by remote or timer.

articles.headline: India recorded 5.89 million crimes in 2024 as crime rate falls
articles.summary: According to NCRB data, India's crime rate fell to 419 per lakh population in 2024, the lowest since 2019. Total crimes dropped 5.7% from the previous year. While offences against the body and property declined, cybercrimes rose by 17%. Accidental deaths reached a record high of 467,857, driven by road accidents and heatstrokes, while suicide cases remained marginally lower at 170,746.

articles.headline: Vinesh Phogat ineligible for Asian Games trials under WFI selection policy
articles.summary: Double World Championships medalist Vinesh Phogat will miss the Asian Games selection trials scheduled for May 30-31. The WFI policy limits eligibility to medalists from recent national championships and U-20 trials. Phogat, who briefly retired after the Paris Olympics, has not competed in the required events. WFI officials stated the rules will not be modified for individual athletes.

articles.headline: CJI seeks report on controversial exchange between Andhra High Court judge
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant has requested a report from the Andhra Pradesh High Court regarding a viral video of Justice Tarlada Rajasekhar Rao. The judge reportedly ordered an advocate into judicial custody for 24 hours during a hearing. The order was not executed following Bar intervention. Legal bodies expressed shock, citing concerns over judicial temperament and fairness.

articles.headline: Supreme Court clarifies 2023 CEC selection verdict meant to fill vacuum
articles.summary: The Supreme Court observed that its 2023 judgment requiring the CJI to be in the selection panel for the Chief Election Commissioner was only intended to fill a legal vacuum until Parliament enacted a law. The bench noted that once the 2023 Act was passed, the court's earlier directions might not apply. The law currently includes a Cabinet Minister instead of the CJI in the panel.

articles.headline: Supreme Court probes claim that rats ate cash seized in Bihar
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ordered an inquiry into the storage of evidence in Bihar police malkhanas after being 'taken by surprise' by a claim that rodents destroyed currency notes seized in a corruption case. The bench questioned how often seized money is lost due to poor storage, noting it represents a significant revenue loss for the state and impacts the prosecution of crimes.

articles.headline: CBI chargesheets real estate firm AVJ Developers and bankers for fraud
articles.summary: The CBI has filed a charge sheet against Delhi-based AVJ Developers, its directors, and bank officials for a conspiracy that defrauded homebuyers and lenders. The probe involves a builder-banker nexus where loans were disbursed under subvention schemes before project starts. The agency is investigating approximately 50 similar cases nationwide following Supreme Court directions.

articles.headline: Poonch residents remember victims one year after Operation Sindoor shelling
articles.summary: Residents of Poonch, Jammu & Kashmir, marked the first anniversary of Operation Sindoor, recalling the heavy cross-border shelling that killed 28 people. Families of victims described the trauma and economic hardship following the four-day military confrontation between India and Pakistan. Local residents have requested the government to build a memorial for those who lost their lives.

articles.headline: US investigators find fuel cut to engines in 2022 China crash
articles.summary: Data released by US aviation investigators shows that fuel supply to both engines of a China Eastern Airlines flight was cut off before it crashed in March 2022, killing 132. The finding mirrors a preliminary analysis of an Air India flight crash in Ahmedabad last year. Both involved Boeing aircraft, though investigators are still reviewing the cockpit voice recorder for final details.

articles.headline: Indian armed forces initiate multi-billion dollar procurements after Operation Sindoor
articles.summary: Following the anniversary of Operation Sindoor, India has moved to acquire five additional S-400 air defence units, 114 Rafale fighter jets, and thousands of loitering munitions. The military is also developing a national defence shield, Mission Sudarshan Chakra, to protect installations from aerial attacks. The navy also commissioned its third nuclear ballistic missile submarine.

articles.headline: Agniveers prove combat readiness during debut in Operation Sindoor
articles.summary: Top military officials stated that Agniveers, India's youngest soldiers, performed with courage and composure during their first combat exposure in Operation Sindoor last year. Over 3,000 Agniveers manned critical air defence systems and operated specialized equipment, successfully intercepting Pakistani drones and missiles. The performance has helped dispel doubts about the recruitment scheme.

articles.headline: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah asserts Congress government will complete full five-year term
articles.summary: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah reiterated that his government will complete its full term, dismissing speculation about a leadership change. He stated that any decision regarding the CM post rests solely with the Congress high command. Meanwhile, preparations are underway for a major event in Tumakuru on May 20 to mark the completion of three years of the current government's term.

articles.headline: BJP leader DN Jeevaraj takes oath as Sringeri MLA after recount
articles.summary: BJP leader DN Jeevaraj took oath as a member of the Karnataka legislative assembly from Sringeri after a high court-ordered recount of postal ballots. The recount overturned the 2023 victory of Congress's TD Raje Gowda. The ceremony was held at Vidhana Soudha, though the process was marred by political friction as the Speaker denied allegations of deliberately delaying the oath.

articles.headline: BJP prepares for Yediyurappa’s Abhimanotsava celebration with over 10 lakh supporters
articles.summary: The BJP is organizing a massive celebration, 'Abhimanotsava', for former Karnataka CM BS Yediyurappa on May 9. Party president BY Vijayendra stated that over 10 lakh supporters and a thousand religious heads are expected to attend. The schedule was adjusted to accommodate Union Home Minister Amit Shah's visit. The event aims to demonstrate the veteran leader's continued popularity.

articles.headline: FIR registered in Bengaluru over massive SMS spoofing fraud claim
articles.summary: Bengaluru police filed an FIR against unknown persons for sending fraudulent messages in the name of an e-commerce platform. The scam, which targeted Hiveloop Technology, promised cash rewards to dupe users. The technology company reported that the spoofing disrupted its operations between April 23 and April 27. Authorities have launched a detailed investigation into the network.

articles.headline: Arrest warrant issued for BJP MLA BP Harish in caste-abuse case
articles.summary: A special court in Bengaluru issued an arrest warrant for BJP MLA BP Harish after he failed to appear for hearings related to a 2023 caste-based abuse case. Harish is accused of making derogatory remarks against the Dalit community during assembly election celebrations. The court directed police to produce him by May 7 following his repeated absence despite multiple summons.

articles.headline: Deepfake video of Finance Minister used to scam Belagavi senior citizen
articles.summary: A 76-year-old man in Belagavi was cheated of Rs 7.9 lakh in an online investment scam that featured an AI-generated deepfake of Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman. The victim was lured by a video on YouTube endorsing a fraudulent scheme. Police registered a case under the IT Act and warned the public against trusting investment advice from AI-generated content on social media.

articles.headline: Karnataka government revokes suspension of DGP K Ramachandra Rao
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has reinstated 1993-batch IPS officer K Ramachandra Rao, revoking his suspension with immediate effect. Rao was suspended in January 2025 following allegations of conduct unbecoming of a public servant. He has been posted as Director General of Police (Police Manual). His stepdaughter remains accused in a separate high-profile gold smuggling investigation.

articles.headline: Three arrested at Bengaluru airport with drugs worth Rs 4.53 crore
articles.summary: Customs officials at Kempegowda International Airport arrested three passengers arriving from Bangkok for smuggling 12.96 kg of hydroponic ganja. The seized drugs are valued at approximately Rs 4.53 crore. The contraband was found concealed in check-in baggage. The accused have been booked under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Act.

articles.headline: Karnataka technical review meeting focuses on drought and water scarcity management
articles.summary: The Karnataka government convened a high-level meeting to strengthen drought preparedness amid El Niño concerns. Experts recommended prioritizing groundwater recharge and large-scale rainwater harvesting.
