Minister NS Boseraju directed officials to prevent water leakages in irrigation structures and launched the 'Neeriddare Naale' awareness scheme to manage resources effectively.

articles.headline: KSCA expresses disappointment as IPL final moved from Bengaluru to Ahmedabad
articles.summary: The Karnataka State Cricket Association (KSCA) expressed shock after the BCCI moved the IPL 2026 final to Ahmedabad. KSCA had formally requested to host the playoffs at Chinnaswamy Stadium, citing successful match conduct this season. While the BCCI cited operational requirements, state leaders dismissed rumors that local ticket disputes with MLAs influenced the decision.

articles.headline: Caste census to guide future policy and social justice: Siddaramaiah
articles.summary: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah stated that the ongoing caste census will provide the data necessary for future policy decisions and advancing social justice. Addressing a gathering in Mysuru, he noted that this is the first such enumeration since 1931. He committed to using the findings to ensure equitable resource distribution and address disparities within various caste classifications.

articles.headline: TVK functionary arrested for sexual abuse of minor girl in Chennai
articles.summary: Chennai police arrested Dinesh, a local office-bearer of the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), on charges of sexually harassing a 10-year-old girl. The accused allegedly lured the minor into his room in April and threatened her. Following a complaint, he was booked under the POCSO Act and remanded to judicial custody. The incident has drawn attention as the TVK seeks to form a government.

articles.headline: Telangana Minister appeals to farmers to avoid protests over crop procurement
articles.summary: Telangana Civil Supplies Minister N Uttam Kumar Reddy urged farmers not to panic over Rabi produce procurement, claiming the government has taken 'unprecedented measures'. The appeal follows protests across several districts due to delays. The minister stated that 17.28 lakh metric tonnes of paddy have already been procured and over Rs 2,300 crore paid to farmers so far.

articles.headline: Telangana CM seeks early central approval for Hyderabad Metro Phase-II
articles.summary: Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy met Union Minister Manohar Lal Khattar to request early approval for the expansion of the Hyderabad Metro Rail. The proposed Phase-II includes seven corridors covering 122.9 km at an estimated cost of Rs 38,595 crore. The project is planned as a joint venture between the state and central governments to support the city's urban expansion.

articles.headline: TTD trust board to distribute free ritual kits to children
articles.summary: The Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams (TTD) announced the 'Akshara Govindam' scheme to provide free kits for children participating in Aksharabhyasam initiation ceremonies. Each kit includes a writing slate, sacred rice, and devotional pictures. The initiative aims to promote Sanatana Dharma. The board also decided to construct a new administrative building to consolidate all TTD offices.

articles.headline: Masked gang steals ATM containing Rs 4.5 lakh in Andhra Pradesh
articles.summary: A gang of four masked men stole an IDFC First Bank ATM in Dharmavaram, Sri Sathyasai district. The group used ropes to drag the machine and load it into a commercial vehicle in less than six minutes. The ATM contained approximately Rs 4.5 lakh in cash. Local police are tracking the getaway vehicle using CCTV footage after a video of the heist went viral on social media.

articles.headline: Eateries across Kerala shut down to protest rising cooking gas prices
articles.summary: Hotels and restaurants in Kerala remained closed on Wednesday during a state-wide strike led by the KHRA. The protest targeted the continuous increase in commercial LPG prices by oil companies. Association leaders argued that rising fuel costs have made the food industry's survival difficult and urged the government to intervene. Marches were held in front of oil company offices.

articles.headline: Security outside Mamata Banerjee’s residence scaled down after TMC election defeat
articles.summary: Following the TMC's loss in the West Bengal assembly elections, the state security directorate reduced the police presence outside the homes of Mamata and Abhishek Banerjee. Around 450 personnel previously assigned to close proximity duty were withdrawn. The move comes as the outgoing CM has not yet resigned, though many of her political appointees have already stepped down.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi alleges 'vote theft' by one in six BJP lawmakers
articles.summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi claimed that every sixth BJP MP secured their seat through 'vote theft' and alleged the Haryana government consists of 'infiltrators'. He asserted that the BJP would not have won more than 140 seats in a fair election. Gandhi's remarks added to the post-poll friction as the BJP prepares to take power in West Bengal and other states.

articles.headline: Sunetra Pawar resigns from Rajya Sabha after winning Baramati assembly seat
articles.summary: NCP head Sunetra Pawar resigned from her Rajya Sabha membership on Wednesday following her election to the Maharashtra Legislative Assembly from Baramati. Her resignation was accepted by the Rajya Sabha chairman in New Delhi. Pawar expressed gratitude for her brief tenure in the national capital, stating it enriched her understanding of national and international issues.

articles.headline: ECI integrated technology platform hit by 6.8 million cyberattacks on counting day
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India revealed that its ECINET platform faced over 6.8 million malicious hits on the May 4 counting day. The cyberattacks, originating from India and overseas, targeted the results portal with the goal of crashing the system. ECI officials stated that cybersecurity protocols successfully countered the hits, ensuring the integrity of the results.

articles.headline: Bangladesh home minister hopes for no pushbacks following BJP election win
articles.summary: Bangladesh Home Minister Salahuddin Ahmed expressed hope that Indian authorities will not attempt to 'push back' suspected illegal immigrants after the BJP's victory in West Bengal. He stated that border guards have been alerted but expects regional stability. The comments follow campaign rhetoric regarding an influx of people from across the border into Indian states.

articles.headline: Top bureaucrats and advisors in West Bengal resign after TMC defeat
articles.summary: Several former chief secretaries and senior advisors appointed by the Mamata Banerjee administration have tendered their resignations following the TMC's loss. Those stepping down include Alapan Bandyopadhyay, HK Dwivedi, and Manoj Pant. Economist Abhirup Sarkar also resigned, stating that as his appointments were political, he had no moral right to continue under the new regime.

articles.headline: Outgoing Chief Ministers of Bengal, Tamil Nadu, and Kerala review poll losses
articles.summary: Outgoing CMs Mamata Banerjee, MK Stalin, and Pinarayi Vijayan spent Wednesday reviewing their parties' assembly election defeats. Banerjee held a meeting with TMC MLAs in Kolkata, while Stalin met with allies and received a visit from actor Rajinikanth. In Kerala, Vijayan attended a CPI(M) secretariat meeting. All three parties are grappling with significant seat losses in their respective states.

articles.headline: High-powered panel to meet on May 12 to select next CBI Director
articles.summary: A committee comprising the Prime Minister, the Chief Justice of India, and the Leader of the Opposition is expected to meet on May 12 to appoint the next CBI Director. A list of senior IPS officers from the 1989-1992 batches is being prepared by the DoPT. Strong contenders include current R&AW chief Parag Jain and Maharashtra DGP Sadanand Date. The current director's extension ends soon.

articles.headline: Six missing after boat capsizes in Yamuna river in Uttar Pradesh
articles.summary: Six people, mostly children aged between 5 and 11, are missing after a boat carrying 10 passengers capsized in the Yamuna river in Hamirpur district. The group was returning from a wedding when the boat lost balance. UP CM Yogi Adityanath has directed local administration and police to carry out relief and rescue operations on a war footing to locate the victims.

articles.headline: Supreme Court issues contempt notice to Education Secretary over university audit
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has issued a contempt notice to the Union Education Secretary for failing to provide logistical support to a committee auditing private universities. The court expressed dissatisfaction after learning that the assigned stenographer lacked sufficient English skills to assist the panel. The secretary must appear in person on May 18 to explain the non-compliance.

articles.headline: One woman dies and five fall ill after C-section surgeries in Rajasthan
articles.summary: Six women developed severe kidney infections following caesarean deliveries at Kota Medical College Hospital, resulting in one death. Families of the victims have alleged medical negligence. The hospital and the state health department have formed committees to investigate the cause of the sudden drop in blood pressure and respiratory issues experienced by the patients.

articles.headline: Film producer and Super Good Films founder RB Choudary dies in accident
articles.summary: Renowned film producer RB Choudary died at age 76 after his car crashed into a wall on a highway in Rajasthan. The accident occurred near Jhoontha village when the vehicle swerved to avoid cattle. Choudary produced popular films in multiple Indian languages. Tributes from South Indian film stars, including Vijay and Rajinikanth, poured in following the news of his demise.

articles.headline: Odisha tribal man receives nearly Rs 15 lakh in donations after viral video
articles.summary: Jitu Munda, an Odisha man who carried his sister's skeletal remains to a bank to prove her death, has received approximately Rs 15 lakh in donations. Major contributors include Physics Wallah founder Alakh Pandey and local politicians. The district administration has also provided him with electricity and a monthly pension, though the tribal community required a purification ritual for his actions.

articles.headline: Greenfield expressway connecting Varanasi to Kolkata cleared despite forest impact
articles.summary: The Union environment ministry's expert committee cleared a 235-km expressway connecting Varanasi to Kolkata, requiring the diversion of over 103 hectares of forest land in West Bengal. The project, estimated at Rs 9,250 crore, traverses tiger landscapes and elephant corridors. To mitigate impact, the NHAI has proposed 20 wildlife underpasses, each at least 300 metres long.

articles.headline: Maratha descendants challenge NCERT's removal of historical empire map in HC
articles.summary: Descendants of royal Maratha families have filed a PIL in the Bombay High Court challenging the removal of a map showing the Maratha empire at its 1759 peak from Class 8 textbooks. The petitioners argued the move by NCERT is arbitrary and perpetuates historical inaccuracy. The map was reportedly removed following objections from Rajput families in Rajasthan regarding city depictions.

articles.headline: UP Minister claims English nursery rhymes teach children to be dishonest
articles.summary: Uttar Pradesh Higher Education Minister Yogendra Upadhyaya stoked controversy by claiming that the popular nursery rhyme 'Johnny, Johnny! Yes Papa' teaches children to lie about eating sugar. He contrasted English poems with Hindi ones, which he believes carry better life lessons. The minister urged teachers to incorporate Indian cultural values and traditional teaching models in schools.

articles.headline: CBI conducts nationwide raids in cyber slavery and human trafficking probe
articles.summary: The CBI carried out searches across nine locations in Mumbai, New Delhi, and Uttar Pradesh, arresting one individual for trafficking Indians to South-East Asia for cyber-slavery. Agents allegedly lured victims with fake job offers. Financial analysis tracked cryptocurrency transactions used in the operation. Electronic devices containing evidence of the organized network were seized.

articles.headline: Iran enforces new shipping protocols for passage through Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Iran’s IRGC announced that 'safe passage' through the Strait of Hormuz is now contingent on new protocols, including registration with the Persian Gulf Strait Authority. Shipowners must provide cargo values and crew nationalities to gain transit. Despite the announcement, shipping companies remain cautious, citing recent attacks and a lack of official safety guidance from international bodies.

articles.headline: China urges reopening of Hormuz ahead of Xi-Trump summit in Beijing
articles.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi called for the swift restoration of safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz during talks with his Iranian counterpart. The energy crisis is a major concern for Beijing, which depends on the Middle East for 40% of its oil. The meeting suggests coordination between the partners before the upcoming summit between President Xi Jinping and Donald Trump.

articles.headline: France moves nuclear-powered aircraft carrier towards Red Sea and Hormuz
articles.summary: The French aircraft carrier strike group, led by the Charles de Gaulle, is moving south of the Suez Canal into the Red Sea. The deployment prepares for a potential mission to restore navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. This is the first time a European carrier has moved south of Suez during the current conflict, signaling increased naval pressure to reopen the vital oil corridor.

articles.headline: UN warns of humanitarian crisis for 20,000 seafarers stranded in Gulf
articles.summary: The UN’s International Maritime Organization sounded an alarm over approximately 20,000 seafarers trapped aboard vessels in the Persian Gulf due to the naval blockade. Many workers from developing countries face war-zone risks and limited legal protections. Around 1,000 vessels are seeking to evacuate the area, but traffic remains paralyzed amid ongoing military threats and mines.

articles.headline: Pakistan seeks urgent spot market LNG supplies to ease power shortage
articles.summary: Pakistan is looking to purchase liquefied natural gas from the spot market for May delivery as hot weather threatens to stretch the national power grid. The country faces a fuel shortage after regular supplies from Qatar were cut due to regional strikes. The lack of fuel has worsened power plant shortages since the escalation of conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran in February.

articles.headline: Anthropic commits $200 billion to Google Cloud for AI development
articles.summary: AI startup Anthropic has agreed to spend $200 billion with Google Cloud over the next five years as part of a new partnership. The deal underscores the massive infrastructure requirements for training large-scale artificial intelligence models. Anthropic continues to scale its operations to compete in the generative AI market, securing long-term computing resources through this deal.

articles.headline: Indonesia considers e-commerce ban for users under 16 years old
articles.summary: The Indonesian government is weighing a ban on e-commerce for individuals under the age of 16. Communications Minister Meutya Hafid stated the move follows a recent social media prohibition for teenagers. The proposed regulation aims to protect minors in the digital economy as Jakarta tightens oversight of online platforms and their impact on younger populations.

articles.headline: Musk wanted $80 billion for Mars colonization, OpenAI president testifies
articles.summary: OpenAI President Greg Brockman testified that Elon Musk supported turning the startup into a for-profit company only if he had full control to raise $80 billion for Mars colonization. The testimony was part of a trial where Musk is seeking damages, claiming the company abandoned its nonprofit goals. Brockman also alleged that Musk physically threatened him during a 2017 confrontation.

articles.headline: Vivek Ramaswamy wins Republican nomination for Ohio Governor primary
articles.summary: Indian-origin politician Vivek Ramaswamy has won the Republican primary for Ohio Governor, securing an endorsement from Donald Trump. Ramaswamy, who rose to prominence during the 2024 presidential race, will face Democrat Amy Acton in November. If successful, he would be only the third Indian-American to serve as a US state governor. He thanked Trump for his support since day one.

articles.headline: Twelve Indian companies to invest $1.1 billion in US supply chains
articles.summary: Twelve Indian businesses announced $1.1 billion in investments in the US to create jobs and strengthen supply chains. The commitment, made at the SelectUSA summit, is part of a broader trend where Indian firms plan to invest over $20.5 billion across technology and manufacturing. US officials described the partnership as a new phase of economic cooperation between the two democracies.

articles.headline: Indian government subsidy spending reaches 91% of annual target by February
articles.summary: India's major subsidy outgo reached Rs 3.89 trillion in the first 11 months of FY26, hitting 91% of the revised estimate. Food and urea subsidies saw significant pressure, with urea already breaching its annual target. Analysts warned that the ongoing West Asia conflict could further escalate costs, potentially requiring upward budget revisions for the coming fiscal year.

articles.headline: NPS Vatsalya pension scheme sees weak start with 2.25 lakh enrolments
articles.summary: The National Pension System's minor account scheme, NPS Vatsalya, has seen a muted response since its September 2024 launch, with only 2.25 lakh accounts opened. Public sector banks like SBI led the sign-ups, while private sector traction remains low. The PFRDA plans a mission-mode strategy to boost awareness and pension penetration, which currently accounts for only 4.5% of India's GDP.

articles.headline: Sunrisers Hyderabad beat Punjab Kings by 33 runs after fielding errors
articles.summary: Sunrisers Hyderabad posted a total of 235/4 after Punjab Kings dropped several key catches, including Ishan Kishan and Heinrich Klaasen. Kishan scored 55 and Klaasen added a quickfire 69. Despite a resilient century from Punjab's Cooper Connolly, the target proved too steep as Pat Cummins took two wickets. The win keeps Sunrisers in a strong position on the IPL points table.

articles.headline: India accepts invitation to play in first FIFA Asean Cup
articles.summary: The All India Football Federation (AIFF) has confirmed India's participation in the first FIFA Asean Cup scheduled for September-October. The tournament features 14 teams split into two groups, offering a $1 million winners' prize. The move is part of AIFF's efforts to provide more international exposure to the national team during the upcoming FIFA international windows.

articles.headline: Gukesh defeats World Championship challenger Sindarov in Warsaw Rapid chess
articles.summary: World champion D Gukesh defeated Javokhir Sindarov in the Grand Chess Tour Super Rapid and Blitz in Warsaw. This was their first meeting since Sindarov was confirmed as Gukesh's challenger for the world title later this year. Gukesh held his nerve under time pressure to force a resignation from Sindarov, moving up to six points in the tournament rankings after six rounds.

articles.headline: Punjab FC rise to sixth in ISL after 1-0 win over Chennaiyin
articles.summary: Punjab FC secured a 1-0 victory against Chennaiyin FC at Delhi's Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium, moving to sixth place in the ISL standings. An own goal by Chennaiyin's Lalrinliana Hnamte in the 84th minute decided the match. The win keeps Punjab's playoff hopes alive as they move within two points of third-placed Mohun Bagan, while Chennaiyin drops to 12th in the 14-team league.

articles.headline: SpiceJet and Akasa seen as frontrunners for ECLGS 5.0 airline funds
articles.summary: SpiceJet and Akasa Air are positioned to access airline-specific funds under the fifth edition of the Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme (ECLGS). The Union Cabinet approved the scheme to support credit flow with up to 100% government guarantees. Individual airlines may access up to ₹1,000 crore, with tenures of seven years, to cushion the impact of high fuel costs and liquidity stress.

articles.headline: Government to retain pari passu funding for India Semiconductor Mission 2.0
articles.summary: The Centre will maintain the 'on equal footing' funding model for the upcoming phase of the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM 2.0). Government incentives will be released in proportion to actual company expenditure rather than being front-loaded. Investment commitments under the first phase reached ₹1.64 lakh crore, surpassing the original target as India builds a domestic chip ecosystem.

articles.headline: Interio by Godrej aims for 25% revenue growth in FY27
articles.summary: Interio by Godrej, the furniture arm of Godrej Enterprises, is targeting a 25% revenue increase in the next fiscal year. The company recorded ₹4,000 crore in revenue for FY26 and plans to reach a ₹10,000 crore topline by 2029. Growth is expected from both institutional workspace designs and retail consumer sales as Indians spend more on home improvements.

articles.headline: Cement makers anticipate demand pick-up following sluggish April
articles.summary: Cement producers expect a sharp recovery in demand this month after labor shortages linked to elections slowed activity in April. Companies are implementing price hikes of ₹10-20 per bag to offset rising petcoke and logistics costs. Analysts suggest the industry may grow by 5-5.5% in FY27, supported by a push in institutional infrastructure and real estate ahead of the monsoon.

articles.headline: Blue Star reports 3.6% revenue growth for fiscal year 2026
articles.summary: Blue Star recorded consolidated revenue from operations of ₹12,402 crore for FY26. However, net profit declined to ₹527 crore from ₹591 crore in the previous year, impacted by a one-time exceptional charge of ₹38.83 crore related to new labour codes. The company achieved its highest-ever quarterly revenue in Q4FY26 at ₹4,072 crore, with operating margins improving to 8%.

articles.headline: CG Power FY26 profit rises 23% on strong power systems growth
articles.summary: CG Power and Industrial Solutions reported an annual net profit of ₹1,198 crore for FY26. The power systems segment saw 46% revenue growth, reflecting strong execution. The company's unexecuted order backlog stood at ₹17,107 crore. Its subsidiary, CG Semi, unveiled one of India's first semiconductor assembly facilities in Gujarat, which is expected to scale up capacity significantly by 2027.

articles.headline: Godrej Consumer Products annual revenue crosses ₹15,000 crore milestone
articles.summary: Godrej Consumer Products (GCPL) reported an 11% rise in Q4 revenue to ₹3,900.4 crore, crossing the ₹15,000 crore annual revenue mark for the first time. Net profit for the quarter rose 9.7%. While home care grew 12%, personal care remained subdued. The company noted that hyperinflation in Argentina impacted margins, but strong performance in Africa helped offset weakness in Indonesia.

articles.headline: Rane (Madras) consolidated net profit soars fivefold in Q4
articles.summary: Auto component maker Rane (Madras) reported a consolidated net profit of ₹37 crore for the fourth quarter, up from ₹6.5 crore a year ago. Total revenue increased 16% to ₹1,052 crore. The surge was supported by an 11% growth in domestic OEM sales and a 27% increase in international sales. The board recommended a final dividend of ₹16 per share as the company expands its steering and brake components.

articles.headline: Arvind Fashions returns to profitability with ₹47 crore Q4 profit
articles.summary: Denim and casual wear player Arvind Fashions reversed a ₹93-crore loss from the previous year to post a net profit of ₹47 crore in Q4FY26. Revenue rose 15% to ₹1,365 crore, driven by strong growth in direct-to-consumer channels and core brands. For the full year, net profit stood at ₹123 crore. The board recommended a final dividend of ₹1.60 per share for the fiscal year.

articles.headline: LG India expands export capabilities under Make in India strategy
articles.summary: LG Electronics India aims to increase its export share, currently at 7% of revenue, by leveraging its local manufacturing base. The company launched its 2026 Home Appliances range, including side-by-side refrigerators manufactured in India. Its 'Essential Series' is expected to reach 22 countries across Asia and Africa this year. LG is mitigating raw material costs through higher localization.

articles.headline: Reliance to invest ₹1.08 lakh crore in Vizag data centre
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh State Investment Promotion Board (SIPB) has approved Reliance's proposal to set up a data centre in Visakhapatnam with an investment of ₹1.08 lakh crore. This marks the second major data centre project in the state after Google Cloud's AI hub. The board also cleared a Royal Enfield plant in Tirupati and an Adani Energy pumped storage project worth ₹12,000 crore.

articles.headline: India likely to maintain conditional support for WTO e-comm moratorium
articles.summary: India is expected to continue its conditional support for extending the WTO moratorium on e-commerce duties, provided it aligns with the TRIPS non-violation complaint moratorium. While the US seeks a permanent extension, Brazil and Turkey favor a two-year term. India remains concerned about potential revenue losses for developing nations, estimated at $10 billion per year.

articles.headline: NMDC increases iron ore prices by ₹200 per tonne
articles.summary: State-owned NMDC has announced an immediate price hike of ₹200 per tonne for both iron ore lump and fines. The price for Baila lump ore has been fixed at ₹5,500 per tonne, while fines are priced at ₹4,700 per tonne. The price adjustment by India's largest iron ore miner was disclosed in a regulatory filing on Wednesday.

articles.headline: S&P Global lowers India FY27 growth forecast to 6.6 percent
articles.summary: S&P Global and Crisil have trimmed India's growth forecast for the current fiscal by 50 basis points to 6.6%. The revision follows external economic shocks including energy supply disruptions and rising oil prices. The report, 'India Forward', suggests that while winter crops might face fertilizer shortages due to the West Asia crisis, domestic pump price stability may cushion retail inflation.

articles.headline: Google Translate celebrates 20 years with expanded language pairs
articles.summary: Google Translate marks its 20th anniversary, now supporting nearly 250 languages and over 60,000 language pairs. The app has introduced pronunciation practice and AI-driven contextual alternatives. India has emerged as a key market, with Hindi, Bengali, and Malayalam among the most frequent translation picks. The tool now supports endangered and indigenous languages globally.

articles.headline: OnePlus Pad 4 launched in India starting at ₹54,999
articles.summary: OnePlus has unveiled its flagship tablet, the OnePlus Pad 4, featuring the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 platform. The device boasts a 13.2-inch 3.4K display with a 144Hz refresh rate and a massive 13,380 mAh battery. Built for professionals and creators, the tablet includes 80W fast charging and an eight-speaker spatial audio setup. Sales commenced on May 5.

articles.headline: POCO introduces C81 series entry-level smartphones for budget users
articles.summary: POCO has launched its C81 and C81x smartphones, targeting users seeking large displays and long battery life at budget prices. Both feature 6.9-inch HD+ screens with 120Hz refresh rates and batteries claimed to last up to two days. Running on Xiaomi HyperOS 3, the devices are priced starting at ₹9,999 and include features like splash resistance and expandable storage up to 2 TB.

articles.headline: India emerges as largest user base for OpenAI ChatGPT Images 2.0
articles.summary: According to OpenAI, India is now the largest user base for its ChatGPT Images 2.0 model. The image generator is being widely used by young creators for anime-style portraits, cinematic headshots, and structured visuals like fashion mood boards. The model supports multilingual input and can generate complex layouts including readable text for fictional newspaper pages and blueprints.

articles.headline: Actual ODI outflow surges 84% over the last two fiscal years
articles.summary: India's actual overseas direct investment (ODI) outflows reached $26.7 billion at the end of FY26, up from $14.5 billion in FY24. Singapore, the US, and Mauritius were the top destinations. The financial and business services sector accounted for 45% of total outflows. Analysts attribute the surge to a weakening rupee and the structural consolidation of global operations by Indian industry.

articles.headline: Indian pharmaceutical companies plan $20.5 billion investment in US
articles.summary: At the 2026 SelectUSA Investment Summit, Indian industry announced plans to invest over $20.5 billion in the United States. Pharmaceutical firms led the charge, with Sun Pharma alone accounting for $11.75 billion for its acquisition of Organon & Co. Other significant investors include Aurobindo Pharma, Biocon, and Zydus Lifesciences, creating thousands of American jobs across multiple sites.

articles.headline: SEBI proposes linking stockbroker net worth to client numbers
articles.summary: SEBI has issued a consultation paper proposing a new formula for broker net worth requirements. The regulator suggests linking variable net worth to the number of clients and credit balances instead of daily cash balances. The move aims to curb aggressive client acquisition risks, particularly through 'authorized persons,' and slow the frenetic pace of retail entry into equity markets.

articles.headline: RBI deliberates compensation and safeguards to curb digital payment fraud
articles.summary: The RBI is considering a draft liability framework that could offer one-time compensation of up to ₹25,000 for victims of authorized push payment (APP) fraud. The central bank is also proposing preventative measures like a 'kill-switch' for digital payments and lower limits for first-time transfers to trusted persons, encouraging a 'stop and think' approach for transactions.

articles.headline: AI impact analysis suggests potential wipeout of entry-level white-collar jobs
articles.summary: Experts warn that agentic AI could drive unemployment among knowledge workers as high as 20% in fields like coding and finance. In India, Cognizant has announced 15,000 layoffs, and Freshworks is using AI for half its code. The shift poses a challenge for India's workforce as IT services and back-office roles, which traditionally absorbed millions, are increasingly automated at source.

articles.headline: App-based monitoring proposed to drive safety for gig workers
articles.summary: With the rapid growth of delivery services, experts suggest using integrated technology to monitor gig worker safety. Platforms could track speeding, route compliance, and helmet use, linking safety adherence to performance ratings and incentives. This shared responsibility model aims to reduce the social costs of road accidents and prevent unsafe shortcuts driven by tight delivery targets.

articles.headline: New industrial policy urged for West Bengal following political shift
articles.summary: Economists suggest the new government in West Bengal should move from populist doles to capital asset creation. A proposed industrial policy focuses on regional clusters: electronics in Kharagpur, auto components in Asansol-Durgapur, and logistics in North Bengal. Addressing land value-sharing and digitizing land records are seen as critical to restoring investor confidence in the state.

articles.headline: L&T stock falls on margin pressure and cautious FY27 outlook
articles.summary: Larsen & Toubro shares dropped as much as 4% after reporting a 3% decline in quarterly net profit to ₹5,326 crore. While revenue grew 11%, brokerages flagged near-term execution disruptions and a mixed operating performance. Analysts noted that the company's 'Lakshya 2031' strategy, involving high investments in data centres and semiconductors, could be return-dilutive in the short term.

articles.headline: SEBI introduces regulatory framework for significant mutual fund indices
articles.summary: The regulator will now classify any index benchmarked by mutual fund schemes with a daily average AUM exceeding ₹20,000 crore as a 'significant index.' The initial list includes the BSE Sensex, Nifty 50, and sectoral debt indices. Index providers administering these benchmarks are required to apply for registration within six months to enhance transparency and accountability in governance.

articles.headline: BJP victory in three seats a danger to Kerala politics says CPI
articles.summary: CPI State Secretary Binoy Viswam stated that the BJP winning three seats and finishing second in six constituencies in the Kerala Assembly polls is a serious concern for secular parties. He noted that the Right-wing surge in the state needs careful study, highlighting a substantial leak of votes from the LDF to the UDF that allowed the saffron party to gain a foothold.

articles.headline: Adani Ports to invest ₹13,000 crore for global fleet scale-up
articles.summary: Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone (APSEZ) plans to invest up to ₹13,000 crore by FY31 to scale its marine fleet to 200 vessels. The company is recalibrating its strategy to enter European markets and reduce concentration risks in West Asia following recent geopolitical tensions. The marine business currently accounts for 7% of APSEZ's overall business with a 70% domestic market share.

articles.headline: RBI overhauls foreign exchange dealer rules to allow principal-agent model
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has revamped the authorization framework for forex dealers. Authorized Dealers can now appoint Forex Correspondents (FxC) under a principal-agent model for money changing business. New eligibility criteria require Category-II entities to have a minimum net worth of ₹10 crore. The move aims to streamline current and capital account transactions through incorporated companies.

articles.headline: Freshworks to cut 500 jobs globally amid AI-driven restructuring
articles.summary: SaaS firm Freshworks has announced an 11% workforce reduction, affecting approximately 500 employees. CEO Dennis Woodside stated that over half of the company's code now originates in AI, necessitating a leaner organizational structure. Despite the layoffs, the company reported 14% revenue growth in Q1 reaching $228.6 million and signed the largest deals in its history during the quarter.

articles.headline: ECLGS 5.0 expected to benefit 1.1 crore MSME accounts
articles.summary: State Bank of India's economic research department estimates that the fifth edition of the Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme could provide liquidity support to 1.1 crore MSMEs. The scheme offers an incremental credit flow of ₹2.3 lakh per account. It is also expected to aid the aviation sector, where airlines face high fuel costs and reduced passenger traffic due to global conflicts.

articles.headline: Air India board to review massive losses and cost-cutting measures
articles.summary: The Air India board will meet on Thursday to review FY26 financials, with losses expected to exceed ₹20,000 crore. The management may seek approval for network rationalization, including a 5-10% reduction in international flights to Europe and North America. Cost-saving measures under discussion include workforce benching and potential pay cuts to navigate high fuel prices and airspace curbs.

articles.headline: Meesho narrows Q4 losses by 88 percent as revenue rises
articles.summary: E-commerce platform Meesho reported that its net loss narrowed to ₹166.35 crore in Q4FY26, down from over ₹1,300 crore last year. Revenue grew 47% to ₹3,531.21 crore, supported by a 43% surge in order volumes and net merchandise value. The company attributed the growth to increased user engagement and higher purchase frequency among repeat customers, while employee costs remained stable.

articles.headline: Global Capability Centres shift focus from junior hiring to AI skills
articles.summary: A Nasscom–Zinnov report reveals that India's 2,117 Global Capability Centres (GCCs) are putting entry-level hiring on hold. Companies are redeploying existing employees and prioritizing AI-centric skills, which have seen a 1.5 percentage point demand increase recently. Over 75% of GCCs are expected to evolve into strategic transformation hubs for global parent companies over the next five years.

articles.headline: Central banks turned gold net sellers in March for first time
articles.summary: For the first time in 10 months, global central banks were net sellers of gold in March, offloading 30 tonnes. Turkiye led the sales with 60 tonnes to manage domestic liquidity, while Russia sold 6 tonnes to cover budget deficits. Conversely, Poland and China remained top buyers, with the People's Bank of China picking up 5 tonnes for its 17th consecutive month of purchases.

articles.headline: CACP recommends revising ethanol prices following sugarcane FRP increase
articles.summary: The Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) has mooted a revision in ethanol prices derived from sugarcane feedstocks after the government raised the FRP to ₹365 per quintal. The panel also suggested a dual pricing mechanism for sugar, separating domestic and commercial users, as the industrial segment accounts for nearly 65% of India's total sugar consumption.

articles.headline: 61 percent chance of El Nino drought conditions before July
articles.summary: The US Climate Prediction Center has warned of a 61% chance of El Nino emerging between May and July. This raises significant concerns for India's South-West monsoon, as El Nino is typically associated with drought. The India Meteorological Department has already forecast below-normal rainfall at 92% of the long-period average, which could threaten the upcoming kharif and rabi sowing seasons.

articles.headline: Apeda to deploy AI for basmati survey across 4 million hectares
articles.summary: The Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority (Apeda) will conduct India's first AI-based basmati paddy survey covering 4 million hectares. The initiative aims to collect data from 500,000 farmers to enable precise crop assessment and varietal identification. The Indian Rice Exporters' Federation has expressed interest in collaborating for reliable production data.

articles.headline: Investment in UP defence corridors exceeds ₹35,000 crore
articles.summary: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath announced that proposals worth over ₹35,000 crore are being utilized across six defence industrial corridors. Key hubs include Aligarh for small arms, Kanpur for ammunition and textiles, and Lucknow for BrahMos missiles. The state has created a large land bank and policy incentives to support over 21,000 start-ups in AI, robotics, and drones.

articles.headline: Nabard projects ₹5.11 lakh crore credit plan for Andhra Pradesh
articles.summary: NABARD has projected a credit outlay of ₹5.11 lakh crore for Andhra Pradesh in 2026-27, a 20% increase over last year. The plan allocates ₹2.55 lakh crore for agriculture, including crop loans and fisheries, and ₹1.64 lakh crore for MSMEs. Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu urged banks to proactively support the government's efforts to promote the state's horticulture and allied sectors.

articles.headline: Should court spend so much time on religion cases, asks SC
articles.summary: A nine-judge Supreme Court bench, led by CJI D.Y. Chandrachud, questioned if it is prudent for the court to spend extensive time resolving religious disputes. The query arose during hearings on the Sabarimala shrine entry verdict and individual fundamental rights. The court is examining the balance between religious freedom and individual rights of conscience and practice.

articles.headline: TVK+ 5 short, Vijay's oath-taking delayed
articles.summary: Actor-politician Vijay's TVK party fell five seats short of a majority, delaying his planned swearing-in ceremony in Chennai. While TVK sources claim support from adequate MLAs, the Governor is yet to invite them to form the government. The party is reportedly in talks with independent members and smaller groups to bridge the gap in the 234-member assembly.

articles.headline: After Jalandhar, blast near Amritsar cantt
articles.summary: An explosion occurred near the BSF Punjab Frontier headquarters in Amritsar, just three hours after a similar blast was reported in Jalandhar. The blast originated from a two-wheeler parked near the boundary wall. Security agencies have been put on high alert. No casualties or injuries were reported, but officials suspect an ISI conspiracy to destabilize the state.

articles.headline: Murders in India dropped by 2.4% in 2024
articles.summary: According to the latest NCRB data, murders in India decreased by 2.4% in 2024, with 27,049 cases reported compared to 27,721 in the previous year. However, crimes against senior citizens rose by 17%, and crimes against women saw a 1.5% decline. The overall crime rate per lakh population decreased from 448.3 to 418.9, reflecting a slight improvement in the national crime landscape.

articles.headline: Olive Ridley turtle makes round trip from Oman
articles.summary: A satellite-tagged Olive Ridley turtle returned to Indian waters after a 3,500km journey from the coast of Oman. The turtle, named Dhaval Lakshmi, was originally rescued and released from Maharashtra six months ago. Researchers say this is a success story for turtle conservation, showing the migration patterns and survival of rehabilitated marine life across the Arabian Sea.

articles.headline: SMVT terminal falls short of airport-like expectations
articles.summary: Four years after its opening, the Sir M Visvesvaraya Terminal in Bengaluru is facing criticism for poor maintenance and lack of services. Commuters have flagged issues like inadequate cooling, non-functional escalators, and litter. Despite being built at a cost of Rs 314 crore as a premier transport hub, users complain it now resembles a standard railway station rather than an airport.

articles.headline: Elderly woman loses Rs 2 crore to fake NIA sleuths
articles.summary: A 78-year-old woman in Bengaluru was defrauded of Rs 2.1 crore in a 'digital arrest' scam. Fraudsters posing as NIA officials contacted her via video call, claiming she was linked to a terror case involving the PFI. Under threat of arrest, she transferred her life savings and proceeds from a gold loan to multiple accounts. Police have registered a case under the Information Technology Act.

articles.headline: Youth fed 10kg bananas to recover swallowed key
articles.summary: In a bizarre incident in Bengaluru, police fed a 17-year-old boy 10kg of bananas to help him pass a swallowed key naturally. The boy had allegedly swallowed the key to a locker containing stolen items when confronted by police. Under medical supervision, the key was successfully retrieved after 24 hours. The youth was later arrested in connection with a local theft case.

articles.headline: Art of Living completes 45 years; PM to inaugurate Dhyan Mandir
articles.summary: Sri Sri Ravi Shankar's Art of Living foundation celebrated its 45th anniversary with the announcement of a new Dhyan Mandir to be inaugurated by the Prime Minister. The foundation has reached over 180 countries, impacting millions through yoga and meditation. The new facility in Bengaluru aims to serve as a global center for mental well-being and social transformation.

articles.headline: Doctors warn of rising head and neck cancers in early 30s
articles.summary: Oncologists in Bengaluru have noted a worrying trend of head and neck cancers striking individuals in their 30s and 40s. While tobacco and alcohol remain primary causes, doctors cite chronic irritation, HPV infections, and late diagnosis as significant factors. Early symptoms like non-healing ulcers or persistent sore throats should not be ignored, as survival rates are high with early detection.

articles.headline: Bengaluru police tow 600 vehicles daily in new drive
articles.summary: Bengaluru traffic police have resumed intensive towing operations, removing nearly 600 vehicles daily for illegal parking. The drive, restarted on April 5 following previous corruption allegations, uses body-worn cameras and GPS tagging to ensure transparency. Fines range from Rs 500 to Rs 1,000 depending on the vehicle type. The move aims to clear major traffic corridors and junctions.

articles.headline: Trash trouble worsens as contractors oppose new door-to-door plan
articles.summary: Waste management in Bengaluru is suffering as contractors oppose a new door-to-door collection plan by BWSML. Delays have led to garbage piling up on roadsides, creating 'black spots' and unhygienic conditions. The new model requires contractors to be responsible for all collection and processing, but administrative hurdles have prevented the full rollout of the 33-package system.

articles.headline: AI to take data entry work off teachers' shoulders
articles.summary: The Karnataka education department is introducing AI-powered platforms to handle data entry and evaluate descriptive answers for students. Developed with IIT-Dharwad, the system uses large language models to correct handwritten scripts for classes 8 to 12. The initiative aims to reduce teachers' non-teaching workload by 20-30%, allowing them to focus more on classroom instruction.

articles.headline: With Congress govt in Kerala, Bandipur traffic ban in focus
articles.summary: The night traffic ban on NH-766 passing through Bandipur Tiger Reserve is back in the spotlight following the Congress victory in Kerala. While environmentalists want the ban to stay for wildlife protection, political leaders in Kerala are pushing for relief to aid trade and tourism. Karnataka remains committed to the ban, citing the need to safeguard the Western Ghats' ecological integrity.

articles.headline: Karnataka moves to save water as drought looms
articles.summary: Faced with a looming water shortage, the Karnataka government has ordered immediate measures to plug leaks and ensure efficient water management in irrigation and urban sectors. Scientists from IISc have been consulted to develop long-term conservation strategies. The state is also launching a public awareness campaign for rainwater harvesting as the El Nino effect intensifies.

articles.headline: INDIA bloc cracks as Congress snaps 11-year-old DMK ties
articles.summary: The INDIA alliance faced a significant blow as the Congress party in Tamil Nadu ended its 11-year relationship with the DMK. The split followed allegations of 'backstabbing' and a lack of coordination during local body polls. Congress has now extended support to Vijay's TVK, potentially reshaping the state's political landscape and the national opposition's united front against the BJP.

articles.headline: India, Vietnam upgrade ties; focus on trade and defence
articles.summary: India and Vietnam have upgraded their relationship to an 'enhanced strategic partnership,' focusing on defense, connectivity, and trade. Prime Minister Modi and the Vietnamese President discussed cooperation in critical minerals and maritime security in the Indo-Pacific. A new MoU on trade and investment aims to reach $25 billion in annual trade by 2030, strengthening regional stability.

articles.headline: HC: Child's right to travel can't hinge on one parent's consent
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court ruled that a minor's right to travel abroad cannot be restricted solely due to a lack of consent from one parent during a matrimonial dispute. The court directed the Regional Passport Office to issue a short-term passport to a five-year-old girl, allowing her to visit her grandparents in Australia. It emphasized that children should not suffer due to parental acrimony.

articles.headline: NCRB: Rs 55 crore in fake currency seized in 2024
articles.summary: Latest NCRB data reveals that Indian law enforcement agencies seized high-quality fake currency notes with a face value of Rs 54.6 crore in 2024. This marks a 23.1% increase over the previous year. Additionally, 3.62 lakh kg of explosives and various arms were seized. The report highlights the ongoing challenge of counterfeit currency in the economy and its links to organized crime networks.

articles.headline: Shrinking space for Muslim MLAs marks post-2014 era
articles.summary: A study on legislative representation shows a significant decline in the number of Muslim MLAs across India since 2014. In major states like Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, and West Bengal, the percentage of Muslim representatives remains well below their population share. Analysts attribute this to shifting party priorities and the polarization of electoral incentives under the current political climate.

articles.headline: SC questions if interfaith marriage is a religious activity
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has questioned whether marriage between individuals of different faiths should be classified as a 'religious activity' or a civil union. The query came during a hearing on laws restricting interfaith marriages in several states. The court is debating if such laws infringe on the individual right to choose a partner and the fundamental right to freedom of conscience.

articles.headline: Transnational cybercrime: Rs 55 billion lost to overseas scams
articles.summary: A UN report highlights that Indian citizens lost over Rs 5,500 crore to transnational cybercrime syndicates based in Southeast Asia in the last year. These 'pig butchering' scams involve fraudulent investment and dating apps. Scammers operate from high-security complexes in countries like Cambodia and Myanmar, targeting unsuspecting victims through sophisticated social engineering tactics.

articles.headline: India's GCC count crosses 2,100; workforce reaches 2.3 million
articles.summary: The number of Global Capability Centres (GCCs) in India has reached 2,117, employing over 2.3 million people. These centers, which started as back-office hubs, are now driving global innovation in AI, cybersecurity, and cloud transformation. Revenues from GCCs reached $61.4 billion this year, reflecting India's growing importance as a high-value technology and research destination.

articles.headline: US memo to end Iran war echoes scrapped nuclear deal
articles.summary: A one-page US memo has surfaced detailing a 14-point plan to end hostilities with Iran, bearing striking similarities to the 2015 nuclear deal trashed by Donald Trump. The memo proposes a permanent ceasefire and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for sanctions relief. Senior officials describe it as a 'fair deal' aimed at preventing a larger regional conflict.

articles.headline: Mphasis Sues Rival Coforge in US Over Poaching and Client Data
articles.summary: Mphasis Ltd has filed a lawsuit against Coforge Ltd in a Colorado court, accusing it of hiring employees in violation of contracts to gain access to confidential client information. The dispute centers on the Charles Schwab account. Mphasis seeks to restrain former VP Brijesh Khergamker from working for the client at Coforge, alleging an unfair competitive advantage.

articles.headline: HDFC Bank governance review finds no major lapses following chairman resignation
articles.summary: A governance review at HDFC Bank by external law firms is expected to report no major lapses, clearing the way for the reappointment of CEO Sashidhar Jagdishan. The review was triggered by the resignation of former chairman Atanu Chakraborty, who cited 'incongruence' in values. The findings are expected to bring certainty to the lender, whose stability is critical to the Indian economy.

articles.headline: Services PMI Hits 5-Month High in April on Strong Demand
articles.summary: India's services sector activity expanded at its fastest pace in five months in April, with the HSBC Services PMI rising to 58.8. The growth was supported by robust domestic demand and an increase in new export orders. While input costs remained elevated, service providers were able to pass on these costs to consumers, indicating strong pricing power in the market.

articles.headline: India and South Korea to Review Bilateral Trade Agreement in May
articles.summary: India and South Korea are scheduled to hold talks on 25 May to review their Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA). India is seeking to address a rising trade deficit and make the 2010 agreement more contemporary. The review is a key step in strengthening economic ties and revising market access terms between the two major Asian economies.

articles.headline: Samsung crosses $1 trillion valuation on AI chip boom
articles.summary: Samsung Electronics' market valuation topped $1 trillion as AI chip demand quadrupled its share price in a year. It is the second Asian firm after TSMC to reach this milestone. The surge drove South Korea’s Kospi index above the 7,000 mark. Samsung remains a cornerstone of the global AI ecosystem along with SK Hynix, as investors bet on sustained demand for advanced memory.

articles.headline: Zee Entertainment Sues Reliance-Disney Over Alleged Music Copyright Breach
articles.summary: Zee has filed an 1,800-page lawsuit against the Reliance-Disney joint venture, seeking $3 million in damages. Zee alleges the JioStar streaming platform used its copyrighted music over 50 times after licensing deals expired. The court has directed JioStar to ensure no ongoing infringement. The dispute reflects rising tensions over content rights in India's consolidating media market.

articles.headline: Electric vehicle retail sales grow 75% year-on-year in April
articles.summary: Electric passenger vehicle retail sales in India surged to 23,506 units in April 2026, up from 13,421 units last year. Tata Motors led the mass-market segment with 8,543 units, followed by Mahindra & Mahindra and MG Motor. In the two-wheeler segment, TVS Motor Company held the top spot, while the overall EV share in the two-wheeler market moderated to 7.8% following incentive changes.

articles.headline: Paytm Returns to Profit with ₹183 Crore Consolidated Q4 Gain
articles.summary: One97 Communications, parent of Paytm, reported a consolidated profit of ₹183 crore for the March quarter, reversing a year-ago loss. Annual revenue grew 22% to ₹8,437 crore. The company expects to grow faster in FY27 by gaining market share in merchant payments and financial services distribution while maintaining tight control over marketing and software expenses.

articles.headline: CNN founder and television pioneer Ted Turner dies at age 87
articles.summary: Ted Turner, the billionaire media mogul who transformed news by creating CNN and the 24-hour news cycle, has died at 87. Turner was known for his brash personality, ownership of professional sports teams, and philanthropic efforts, including a $1 billion donation to UN charities. US President Donald Trump paid tribute, calling him a great figure in broadcast history.

articles.headline: India and Vietnam set $25 billion trade target by 2030
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Vietnamese President To Lam held talks in New Delhi, elevating ties to an enhanced comprehensive strategic partnership. The nations signed 13 agreements covering trade, defence, and critical minerals. They set a bilateral trade target of $25 billion by 2030. New initiatives will focus on supply chain resilience, digital payments, and pharmaceutical access.

articles.headline: Vijay stakes claim to form Tamil Nadu government with Congress support
articles.summary: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) chief Joseph Vijay met Governor Rajendra Arlekar to stake a claim to form the government after Congress broke its alliance with DMK to support him. TVK and Congress together hold 113 seats, still short of the 118-seat majority in the 234-member house. The Governor is reportedly weighing legal opinions before extending an invitation for the swearing-in.

articles.headline: Bajaj Auto Q4 net more than doubles on record volumes
articles.summary: Bajaj Auto's Q4 consolidated net profit jumped 103% to ₹3,662 crore, supported by record volumes and favorable currency. Revenue rose 41% to ₹17,832 crore. Results include the first-time consolidation of KTM-owner BAIHAG. Domestic motorcycle revenue rose 30%, while the Chetak EV hit a milestone with over 100,000 quarterly retail units despite previous supply constraints.

articles.headline: Indian services sector growth reaches five-month high in April
articles.summary: Growth in India's services sector accelerated to its strongest pace since November 2025, with the HSBC Services PMI rising to 58.8 in April. Domestic demand drove new business inflows, even as export orders softened due to the West Asia conflict. While food and labor costs remain elevated, selling price inflation eased to a three-month low as some firms absorbed higher operating expenses.

articles.headline: Nitish Kumar’s son Nishant likely to join Bihar cabinet expansion
articles.summary: Nishant Kumar, son of former Bihar CM Nitish Kumar, is expected to join the state cabinet under CM Samrat Choudhary. This marks Nishant's formal entry into politics after previously avoiding governmental roles. The move is seen as part of a leadership transition within the JD(U). Smaller allies like HAM and RLM are also expected to secure berths in the upcoming expansion.

articles.headline: Shashi Shekhar Vempati appointed as new chairperson of CBFC
articles.summary: Former Prasar Bharati CEO Shashi Shekhar Vempati has been appointed as the new chairperson of the Central Board of Film Certification (CBFC) for a three-year term. He succeeds lyricist Prasoon Joshi, who recently moved to Prasar Bharati. Vempati, who has extensive experience in media and technology, is expected to bring a new perspective to the film certification body.

articles.headline: Confirmed Hantavirus cases from cruise ship outbreak rise to five
articles.summary: The WHO reported five confirmed hantavirus cases among passengers on a cruise ship anchored near Cape Verde. Three people have died, and three others were evacuated to the Netherlands for treatment. The outbreak is believed to have originated from a couple bird-watching in Argentina before boarding. While 150 people are isolating, experts say the risk to the global population remains low.

articles.headline: Elon Musk to Invest $55 Billion in SpaceX AI Chip Project
articles.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX plans to invest at least $55 billion to build a dedicated semiconductor manufacturing facility in Texas. The plant will produce specialized AI chips for SpaceX's rocket systems and Starlink satellites. The project, known as 'Terafab,' is expected to be a major hub for aerospace and robotics innovation, further reducing the company's reliance on external suppliers.

articles.headline: Brigade Parade Ground to host West Bengal ministerial swearing-in ceremony
articles.summary: Preparations are underway at Kolkata's Brigade Parade Ground for the swearing-in of West Bengal’s first BJP government on May 9. The ceremony, traditionally held at Raj Bhawan, will be attended by PM Narendra Modi and senior Union ministers. This marks the first time a state government will take oath at the historic public venue, which has hosted some of India's largest political rallies.

articles.headline: Himanta Biswa Sarma resigns as Assam CM to form new government
articles.summary: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma submitted his resignation to the Governor to pave the way for a new BJP-led government. He will continue as the caretaker head until the swearing-in ceremony, likely on May 12. Sarma noted that the timing depends on PM Narendra Modi's schedule. The BJP and its allies, AGP and BPF, will soon meet the Governor to formally stake their claim.

articles.headline: Bengal bloodshed: Suvendu's PA killed in car near Kolkata
articles.summary: Chandranath Rath, personal assistant to BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari, was shot dead by bike-borne assailants in his car near Kolkata. Another aide, Buddhadeb Bera, was injured. The incident, occurring 48 hours after poll results, has sparked political outrage. Adhikari has demanded a court-monitored CBI investigation into the killing, which he claims was politically motivated.

articles.headline: Congress and DMK end five-decade alliance in Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: The decades-long partnership between the Indian National Congress and the DMK has collapsed after the AICC extended support to Vijay's TVK to form the government. The DMK termed the move 'backstabbing.' Analysts suggest the split could complicate national-level coordination for the opposition and signals a major political realignment in Tamil Nadu ahead of future local and national polls.

articles.headline: India maintains ban on bilateral sports ties with Pakistan
articles.summary: The Union sports ministry confirmed that India will not participate in bilateral sports events with Pakistan, though it will allow competition in multilateral tournaments. Indian teams will not travel to Pakistan, nor will Pakistani teams be permitted to play in India outside of international governing body events. The policy aligns with India's broader diplomatic stance following recent conflicts.

articles.headline: Arsenal reaches Champions League final for first time since 2006
articles.summary: Arsenal secured a spot in the Champions League final after Bukayo Saka's goal earned them a 1-0 win over Atletico Madrid, winning 2-1 on aggregate. This ends a 20-year wait for the Gunners to reach the final. Manager Mikel Arteta praised the team's belief and the supporters' energy as they prepare for the final in Budapest. The victory also equalled a club record for most wins in a season.

articles.headline: West Asia peace deal talks lift sensex by 941 points
articles.summary: Indian stock markets surged as news of a potential peace deal in West Asia and falling crude oil prices boosted investor sentiment. The Sensex gained 941 points, closing near the 78,000 mark. Analysts suggest that a de-escalation of regional tensions could lead to lower inflation and more stable global supply chains, providing a much-needed rally for domestic financial and industrial sectors.
